## Insertion 6 — Baseline table contract

```tex
\subsection{Baseline coefficient table contract}
\label{app:baseline-coefficient-table-contract}

The baseline tables needed for the current cubic gates are:
\[
G_\pm^{(0)}(z),\qquad
W_\pm(z):=G_\pm^{(0)}(z)^{-1/2},
\qquad
N_0(z):=N^{(0)}(z).
\]
Write
\[
W_\pm(z)=\sum_{n\ge0}W_{\pm,n}z^n,
\qquad
N_0(z)=\sum_{n\ge0}N_0[n]z^n.
\]

For the first live non-\(Y\) gate only the following baseline mixed coefficient
is needed at leading order:
\[
N_0[0].
\]
For the Stage 1 generator and later all-left/all-right audits, one needs the
ordinary-\(z\) baseline tables through the ranges specified in
Appendix~\ref{app:frechet-table-range-contract}.
```

## Insertion 7 — Fréchet table range contract

```tex
\subsection{Fréchet table range contract}
\label{app:frechet-table-range-contract}

The finite Fréchet recurrences of
Section~\ref{sec:finite-frechet-coefficient-calculus} require the following
coefficient ranges for the active UV-026 gates.

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
\text{Object} & \text{Range} & \text{Use}
\\
\hline
\Lambda_\pm^{[1]} & [z^1] & \text{grade-one first Fréchet input}
\\
\Lambda_\pm^{[5]} & [z^5] & \text{grade-five first Fréchet input}
\\
\mathcal L_-^{\{1,1\}} & [z^2] &
L_2N_0R_1\text{ with }(1,1)\text{ on the left}
\\
\mathcal L_-^{\{1,5\}} & [z^6] &
L_2N_0R_1\text{ with }(1,5)\text{ on the left}
\\
\mathcal R_+^{\{1,1\}} & [z^2] &
L_1N_0R_2\text{ with }(1,1)\text{ on the right}
\\
\mathcal R_+^{\{1,5\}} & [z^6] &
L_1N_0R_2\text{ with }(1,5)\text{ on the right}
\\
G^-_{324} & [z^3],\ldots,[z^7] &
L_3N_0R_0\text{ audit, pending}
\\
G^+_{324} & [z^3],\ldots,[z^7] &
L_0N_0R_3\text{ audit, pending}
\end{array}
\]

Here \(\mathcal L\) and \(\mathcal R\) denote the left and right second
Fréchet inverse-square-root outputs in the same fixed \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\)
normalization.  The third-Fréchet rows are diagnostic until the
all-left/all-right homogeneous order audit is completed.
```

## Insertion 8 — First live non-\(Y\) gate table

```tex
\subsection{Leading table for \(L_2N_0R_1/L_1N_0R_2\)}
\label{app:l2n0r1-leading-table}

The first live homogeneous non-\(Y\) gate is
\[
L_2N_0R_1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
L_1N_0R_2.
\]
The leading table is:
\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{Tag vector} & \text{leading fixed-sector coefficient}
\\
\hline
C_{112}^{L_2N_0R_1}
&
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
[z^2]\mathcal L_-^{\{1,1\}}\,
N_0[0]\,
[z^5]\Lambda_+^{[5]}
\right)
\\[1.2ex]
C_{122}^{L_2N_0R_1}
&
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
[z^6]\mathcal L_-^{\{1,5\}}\,
N_0[0]\,
[z^1]\Lambda_+^{[1]}
\right)
\\[1.2ex]
C_{112}^{L_1N_0R_2}
&
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
[z^1]\Lambda_-^{[1]}\,
N_0[0]\,
[z^6]\mathcal R_+^{\{1,5\}}
\right)
\\[1.2ex]
C_{122}^{L_1N_0R_2}
&
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
[z^5]\Lambda_-^{[5]}\,
N_0[0]\,
[z^2]\mathcal R_+^{\{1,1\}}
\right)
\end{array}
\]

Each entry is a fixed-sector vector in
\[
\mathfrak f=\operatorname{span}\{I,S\}.
\]
If an entry is
\[
C=uI+vS
\]
and
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
\]
then the quotient-gauge test is
\[
uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]
The gate closes only if all four tag vectors pass this test.
```

This is the most useful table for agents. It matches the current live-gate reduction: the live non-\(Y\) theorem needs exactly these leading coefficients and determinant tests. turn93file10

```tex
\\
L_0N_0R_3 & G^+_{abc,n}\text{ in the relevant source triples, }3\le n\le7
\end{array}

uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]

Generic matrix algebra, one-pair \(K_5/K_3\) identities, determinant
scalarization, or \(\Phi_K\)-scalar invisibility do not close this table.
```

## Insertion 10 — Agent-facing validation checklist

```tex
\subsection{Validation checklist for coefficient agents}
\label{app:validation-checklist-coefficient-agents}

A valid coefficient-table submission must specify:
\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{Item} & \text{Required declaration}
\\
\hline
\text{ordinary variable} & z\text{ after }s=z/Q^2
\\
\text{stage} & \text{pre-}\Phi_K
\\
\text{source tags} & \tau_1,\tau_2\text{ retained through }112/122
\\
\text{same-point input} & \text{raw vs. }\mathfrak D_Q\text{ convention stated}
\\
\text{mixed input} & M_i^{[a]}=\operatorname{Gr}_a(\mathfrak D_Q\delta N_i^{\lin})
\\
\text{baseline table} & N_0[0]\text{ and needed }W_{\pm,n}
\\
\text{projection} & \pi_{\mathfrak f}\text{ before determinant test}
\\
\text{determinant test} & uv_5-u_5v=0
\end{array}
\]

A submission is not valid if it:
\begin{enumerate}
\item argues after \(\Phi_K\);
\item uses determinant scalarization as the definition of the quotient;
\item replaces the active homogeneous grade by a matrix-output \(M^{[5]}\)
without the no-double-counting theorem;
\item treats recurrence formulas as quotient-gauge proofs without computing the
fixed-sector vectors;
\item omits the baseline \(q_0\)-jet convention or the grade-zero sector.
\end{enumerate}
```

This will directly help agents. It encodes the acceptance criteria from the source/normalization audits. turn93file9

```tex
\begin{remark}[Computational status of the raw package shortcut]

derivative-normalized weights
\[

and the homogeneous Taylor weights
\[

which is the package quotient-gauge statement for
\[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}.

Definition~\ref{def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative};
the finite recurrences and raw gates are a coefficient toolkit, not a closure
of the package theorem.
\end{remark}
```

## Edit 1 — Replace `def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative`

**Replace the current definition with:**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Normalized corrected-whitening fixed-sector package representative]
\label{def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative}
Assume the amplitude-normalized package
\[
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}
\]
extends holomorphically to \(\delta=0\) through ordinary order \(7\).

First extract the moving fixed-sector representative
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h)
:=
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}(h)
\in\mathfrak f_h .
\]
Then define the normalized fixed-target package representative by
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h)
:=
\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}
\left(
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h)
\right)
\in\mathfrak f_m .
\]
Equivalently,
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h)
=
\sum_{j=0}^{7}(h-m)^jB_j+O((h-m)^8),
\qquad
B_j\in\mathfrak f_m.
\]
Although the coefficients still depend on the moving parameter \(h\), the
representatives themselves have already been transported to the fixed
midpoint fiber \(\mathfrak f_m\).

When we write \(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\) below, we mean this normalized
fixed-sector package representative \(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}\), not
the full unprojected matrix cross-effect.
\end{definition}
```

---

## Edit 2 — Replace `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor`

**Replace the current definition with:**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Package-level order-\(7\) quotient extractor]
\label{def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor}
For the normalized fixed-target package representative, define
\[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
[z^7]\,B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\in \mathfrak f_m .
\]
The quotient-valued order-\(7\) extractor is
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
\left[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\right]
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
\end{definition}
```

---

## Edit 3 — Add Fréchet coefficient normalization convention

**Placement:** near the start of `sec:finite-frechet-coefficient-calculus`, after the opening paragraph.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Fr\'echet coefficient normalization]
\label{rem:frechet-coefficient-normalization}
The finite tables in this section use homogeneous Taylor coefficients in the
ordinary \(z\)-expansion of the inverse-square-root transfer.  Thus
\(\Lambda\), \(H\), and \(G\) denote the actual coefficient contributions to
the expanded matrix series, not raw multilinear derivatives \(D,D^2,D^3\)
unless explicitly stated.

Equivalently, if a separate computation uses derivative-normalized tensors,
then the dictionary is
\[
H_{\mathrm{Taylor}}=\frac{1}{2!}H_{\mathrm{deriv}},
\qquad
G_{\mathrm{Taylor}}=\frac{1}{3!}G_{\mathrm{deriv}}.
\]
All determinant tests in this paper use the Taylor-coefficient convention
unless the contrary is explicitly stated.
\end{remark}
```

This addresses the \(D^2\) versus \(D^2/2!\), \(D^3\) versus \(D^3/3!\) ambiguity that agents have repeatedly audited. 

---

## Edit 4 — Add source-weight-linear package-input obligation

**Placement:** as a new first item inside `rem:definition-level-material-and-theorem-obligations`.

```tex
\item \textbf{Source-weight-linear package input.}
One must prove that the tagged pre-whitening source block induces the actual
source-weight-linear input used by the corrected-whitening cross-effect.  In
particular, the construction must not reintroduce the rejected signed-lift
shortcut in which multiplying an even whitened package by the source amplitude
leaves higher \(a^3,a^5,\ldots\) terms.  The package input must be invariant,
functorial through the corrected-whitening transfer, and compatible with
the quotient extractor \(Q_{7,m}^q\).
```

This makes UV-022 visible as a theorem obligation inside the package layer rather than leaving it only in the UV ledger. 

---

## Edit 5 — Add residual-source tower guardrail

**Placement:** after `rem:grade-zero-sector-remains-open`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Residual-source tower before highest-new reduction]
\label{rem:residual-source-tower-before-highest-new-reduction}
The homogeneous scalar-grade convention organizes the local residual source
tower, but it does not by itself identify all residual-source coefficients with
the highest-new tower \(q^{(7)}\).  Finite quotient-gauge identities must first
be tested in the residual-source variables supplied by the corrected source
block.  Only a residual that survives this package-level test should be passed
to the later same-tower / highest-new reduction.

Thus the statement that the eventual local bridge should run through the
single highest-new source \(q^{(7)}\) is a downstream package-to-jet bridge
claim.  It is not a substitute for the pre-\(\Phi_K\), pre-determinant
fixed-sector quotient-gauge calculation.
\end{remark}
```

This prevents agents from prematurely invoking same-tower closure before the package-level residual-source test has actually been run. 

---

## Edit 6 — Add representative-choice guardrail for lower \(B_j[M]\) tables

**Placement:** after `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Representative choices and lower coefficient tables]
\label{rem:representative-choices-lower-coefficient-tables}
The lower coefficients \(B_j[M]\) are representative-dependent as elements of
\(\mathfrak f_m\), but the order-\(7\) connection coordinate
\[
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i)
\]
is independent of changes of \(B_j[M]\) by multiples of \(A_m\).  Therefore
coefficient tables may choose any analytic section used by
Definition~\ref{def:representative-level-midpoint-trivialization}, but they
must keep that section fixed until the final quotient-coordinate calculation.
Changing representatives in the middle of a finite table is not a valid
simplification.
\end{remark}
```

This follows directly from the representative-level midpoint-trivialization setup: the quotient coordinate is invariant, but intermediate \(B_j[M]\)-tables are section-dependent and must be handled consistently. 

---

## Edit 7 — Optional UV-026 drift guardrail

**Placement:** append to `rem:current-uv-chain-and-status`.

```tex
In this formulation, UV-026 is a theorem about the normalized corrected
package representative.  The raw seven-family Fr\'echet sum is only a
diagnostic object; its failure under a matrix-output \(M^{[5]}\) convention
is recorded in Remark~\ref{rem:computational-status-raw-package} and does not
replace the corrected package theorem.
```

This is optional, but useful. It prevents the paper from drifting back to “UV-026 = raw seven-family cancellation,” which the current package architecture explicitly rejects.

I would add or revise the following.

**1. Mark WIP Lemmas As Audited WIP**

Current issue: `lem:transpose-parity-source-linear-mixed-input` and `lem:y-slot-families-absent-homogeneous-grade` are stated as lemmas without proof. Since they are UV/WIP, agents may over-promote them.

Insert after `rem:scope-y-slot-absence-homogeneous-grade`:

```tex
\begin{remark}[Status of the homogeneous-grade lemmas]
\label{rem:status-homogeneous-grade-lemmas}
Lemmas~\ref{lem:transpose-parity-source-linear-mixed-input}
and~\ref{lem:y-slot-families-absent-homogeneous-grade} record audited
current working facts from the UV-026 coefficient push.  They are intended as
briefing-grade inputs for the corrected package program, not as final closure
of UV-026.  Any paper-level promotion still requires the full source theorem
fixing the scalar-grade convention, the baseline \(q_0\)-jet table, the
grade-zero sector, and the live non-\(Y\) fixed-sector determinant tests.
\end{remark}
```

**2. Add A Short Proof Sketch For The Parity Lemma**

Insert after `lem:transpose-parity-source-linear-mixed-input`:

```tex
\begin{proof}[Proof sketch / current verification]
Changing \(z\) to \(-z\) interchanges the two endpoints in the scaled mixed
block.  The mixed block is therefore transformed by endpoint swap, which is
matrix transpose in the left/right basis.  Hence the source-linear mixed input
satisfies \(M(-z)=M(z)^T\).  The diagonal and symmetric off-diagonal channels
are even, while the antisymmetric off-diagonal channel is odd.  The audited
order-\(7\) expansion places \([z^5]M\) in that antisymmetric channel, supported
by \(r^{(2)}(m),r^{(4)}(m),r^{(6)}(m)\), not by the homogeneous grade-five
source \(r^{(7)}(m)\).
\end{proof}
```

**3. Clarify The Two Different “Order 7” Notions**

Current issue: `B_7^{\mathfrak f}` is an ordinary-\(z\) coefficient, while later `B_j[M]` are moving \((h-m)^j\)-coefficients. This is easy for agents to confuse.

Insert before `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients`:

```tex
\begin{remark}[Two coefficient indices]
\label{rem:two-coefficient-indices-package}
The subscript \(7\) in \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) denotes the ordinary \(z\)-order
coefficient extracted before quotienting.  By contrast, the coefficients
\(B_j[M]\) below are coefficients in the moving midpoint expansion in
\((h-m)^j\) of a fixed residual monomial \(M\).  These are different indices:
the connection correction uses lower moving coefficients \(B_{7-i}[M]\) to
correct the quotient line at ordinary order \(7\).
\end{remark}
```

**4. Back-Capture The Connection-Correction Subtarget In Main UV Status**

The appendix has a good connection-correction package, but the main UV chain barely advertises it. Add to `rem:current-uv-chain-and-status`, after the UV-026 bullet:

```tex
\item \textbf{Connection correction subtarget.}  For the normalized moving
package representative, compute the lower fixed-sector coefficients
\[
A_i=[(h-m)^i]A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),\qquad 1\le i\le7,
\]
and
\[
B_j[M_A],\ B_j[M_C],\qquad 0\le j\le6,
\]
for
\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]
The required cancellation identities are
\[
R_7^{(A)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\qquad
R_7^{(C)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]
These are package-representative identities, not raw seven-family identities.
```

**5. Add An Explicit “What This Does Not Solve” Paragraph**

The two-point / mixed four-point map is good, but it should protect against overclaim.

Insert before the end of `rem:corrected-package-map-two-point-four-point`:

```tex
This implication does not by itself prove that the scalar exits
\(T_v=0\) or \(\mathcal R=0\) are impossible.  Its role is narrower: it removes
the possibility that non-\((1,1)\) corrected source terms create an independent
fixed-sector quotient direction.  After that removal, the existing mixed
four-point capture theorem reduces every clean mixed branch to the scalar exits,
and the two-point residual is pushed into the midpoint quotient edge package.
```

**7. Consolidate Duplicate Negative Shortcut Remarks**

`rem:negative-shortcuts-not-to-reuse-package-frontier` and `rem:shortcut-routes-excluded-current-package-theorem` overlap heavily. Keep one consolidated remark. Suggested replacement heading/body:

```tex
\begin{remark}[Shortcut routes excluded for the corrected package theorem]
\label{rem:shortcut-routes-excluded-current-package-theorem}
The following routes do not prove the corrected package quotient theorem:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \(\Phi_K\)-scalar invisibility or scalar hiding after \(\Phi_K\).
\item Determinant scalarization used as the definition of the quotient class.
\item Swap symmetry or one-pair \(K_5/K_3\) gauge laws without the actual
two-atom package object.
\item The tagged pre-whitening source block alone.
\item The raw seven-family Fr\'echet sum; tested source-constrained
nonconstant baselines leave nonzero determinant residuals.
\item Cubic/quintic edge laws plus the abstract source criterion alone.
\item Existing one-pair order-\(7\) or centered \(D_2\) formulas promoted
directly to a two-atom order-\(7\) Hessian.
\item Signed source-weight lift alone; it restores oddness but leaves higher
\(a^3,a^5,\ldots\) amplitude terms.
\end{enumerate}
Every positive route must work before \(\Phi_K\), before determinant
scalarization, and inside the fixed-target quotient package.
\end{remark}
```

**Bottom Line**

The draft is now mostly aligned with the current research push. The main gaps are not missing UV-026 content; they are status hygiene and overclaim prevention: add WIP provenance, clarify the two coefficient indices, promote the connection-correction subtarget into the main UV map, and remove duplicate/stale negative-shortcut wording.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Status of the homogeneous-grade lemmas]
\label{rem:status-homogeneous-grade-lemmas}
Lemmas~\ref{lem:transpose-parity-source-linear-mixed-input}
and~\ref{lem:y-slot-families-absent-homogeneous-grade} record audited
current working facts from the UV-026 coefficient push.  They are intended as

fixing the scalar-grade convention, the baseline \(q_0\)-jet table, the
```

# Edit 2 — Add verification note for transpose parity lemma

**Placement:** immediately after `lem:transpose-parity-source-linear-mixed-input`.

I would use a `remark`, not a `proof`, because the result is still part of the audited WIP layer.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Verification sketch for the transpose-parity mixed input]
\label{rem:verification-sketch-transpose-parity-mixed-input}
Changing \(z\) to \(-z\) interchanges the two endpoints in the scaled mixed
block.  The mixed block is therefore transformed by endpoint swap, which is
matrix transpose in the left/right basis.  Hence the source-linear mixed input
satisfies
\[
M(-z)=M(z)^T.
\]
The diagonal and symmetric off-diagonal channels are even, while the
antisymmetric off-diagonal channel is odd.  The audited order-\(7\) expansion
places \([z^5]M\) in that antisymmetric channel, supported by
\[
r^{(2)}(m),\qquad r^{(4)}(m),\qquad r^{(6)}(m),
\]
not by the homogeneous grade-five source \(r^{(7)}(m)\).
\end{remark}
```

This captures the worker’s proposed proof sketch while keeping status discipline. The proposal itself correctly identified that the current lemmas need WIP/provenance context. 

---

# Edit 3 — Clarify the two different coefficient indices

**Placement:** immediately before `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Two coefficient indices]
\label{rem:two-coefficient-indices-package}
The subscript \(7\) in \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) denotes the ordinary \(z\)-order
coefficient extracted before quotienting.  By contrast, the coefficients
\(B_j[M]\) below are coefficients in the moving midpoint expansion in
\((h-m)^j\) of a fixed residual monomial \(M\).  These are different indices:
the connection correction uses lower moving coefficients \(B_{7-i}[M]\) to
correct the quotient line at ordinary order \(7\).
\end{remark}
```

This is worth adding. It prevents a recurring confusion: \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) is a \(z^7\)-coefficient, while \(B_j[M]\) is an \((h-m)^j\)-coefficient. The worker’s diagnosis here is correct. 

---

# Edit 4 — Add connection-correction subtarget to the main UV map

**Placement:** inside `rem:current-uv-chain-and-status`, immediately after the UV-026 item and before `\end{enumerate}`.

```tex
\item \textbf{Connection correction subtarget.}
For the normalized fixed-target package representative, compute the moving
line coefficients
\[
A_i=[(h-m)^i]A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),
\qquad
1\le i\le7,
\]
and the lower fixed-sector package coefficients
\[
B_j[M_A],\quad B_j[M_C],
\qquad
0\le j\le6,
\]
for
\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]
The required cancellation identities are
\[
R_7^{(A)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]
and
\[
R_7^{(C)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]
These are package-representative identities, not raw seven-family identities.
```

Even though the appendix/table-contract material already says this, the main UV map should advertise it. The worker correctly notes that the main UV chain barely advertises the connection-correction subtarget.

```tex
T_v=0
```

```tex
\(a^3,a^5,\ldots\) amplitude terms.
\end{enumerate}
Every positive route must work before \(\Phi_K\), before determinant
scalarization, and inside the fixed-target quotient package.
\end{remark}
```

## User (2026-04-24 17:49:00)

ok can you double check these suggestions, and give me the latex? I rechecked the updated draft. You effectively did add the proof-sketch I cared about, just as a `remark` rather than a `proof`:

[proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:12806) has `Verification sketch for the transpose-parity mixed input`.

That is enough. I would not add a formal `proof` environment unless you want the lemma to read as promoted paper theorem rather than WIP/audited briefing material.

Why I wanted it added: the statement

```tex
M(-z)=M(z)^T
```

is doing a lot of load-bearing work. It is the reason `[z^5]M` belongs to the off-diagonal antisymmetric channel and is supported by \(r^{(2)},r^{(4)},r^{(6)}\), not by homogeneous \(r^{(7)}\). Without even a short derivation, agents can easily treat the Y-slot absence as magic, or worse, reopen the stale \(M^{[5]}\) convention. The current verification-sketch remark fixes that.

The one thing I would still change is more important than the proof sketch.

**Potential Issue: Coefficientwise Trivialization**

In [proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:28054), `Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h)` is defined using `\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]`, so it is really a finite \(z\)-polynomial with coefficients in \(\mathfrak f_h\), not just an element of \(\mathfrak f_h\). Then `\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}` should be applied coefficientwise.

Add this inside `def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative` after the definition of `Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h)`:

```tex
If
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}z^nZ_n(h),
\qquad
Z_n(h)\in\mathfrak f_h,
\]
then \(\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}\) is applied coefficientwise:
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
:=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}
z^n\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}Z_n(h)
\in \mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Thus \([z^7]B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)\) is taken after
fixed-target trivialization.
```

Then I would change `B_j\in\mathfrak f_m` to:

```tex
B_j\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
```

unless you intend `B_j` to mean the already extracted ordinary-\(z\) coefficient.

**Potential Issue: Representative Independence Claim**

This remark worries me:

[proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:28184)

It says the connection coordinate is independent of changes of \(B_j[M]\) by multiples of \(A_m\). That is not obviously true termwise, because

```tex
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M]+cA_m,A_i)
```

changes by \(c\,\omega_m(A_m,A_i)\), which need not vanish when the quotient line moves.

I would weaken it to:

```tex
The lower coefficients \(B_j[M]\) are representative-dependent.  The
connection expression is meaningful only after a single analytic
representative/trivialization has been fixed.  It is not invariant under
arbitrary termwise replacements
\[
B_j[M]\mapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
\]
unless the corresponding direct term and moving-line expansion are transformed
coherently.  Thus coefficient tables may choose any analytic section from
Definition~\ref{def:representative-level-midpoint-trivialization}, but must keep
that section fixed through the final quotient-coordinate calculation.
```

That is the main hardening I’d do next.

Everything else looks aligned: no duplicate labels, no missing `\ref`/`\eqref` targets, and the old stale `L_1YR_1`-first-live story has been corrected.

2. **Fix the package representative definitions.** The coefficientwise trivialization issue and representative-choice issue are real. The current \(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h)\) object should be treated as a finite \(z\)-polynomial after fixed-sector extraction, and \(\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}\) should act coefficientwise. Also, the old claim that the connection coordinate is independent under arbitrary termwise shifts \(B_j[M]\mapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m\) is too strong, because \(\omega_m(A_m,A_i)\) need not vanish.

---

## Edit 1 — Refine `def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative`

**Replace the current definition with this version.**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Normalized corrected-whitening fixed-sector package representative]
\label{def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative}
Assume the amplitude-normalized package
\[
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}
\]
extends holomorphically to \(\delta=0\) through ordinary order \(7\).

First extract the moving fixed-sector \(z\)-polynomial
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
:=
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}(h)
\in
\mathfrak f_h[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Write
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}z^nZ_n(h),
\qquad
Z_n(h)\in\mathfrak f_h .
\]
The midpoint trivialization is applied coefficientwise:
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
:=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}
z^n\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}Z_n(h)
\in
\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Thus ordinary \(z\)-coefficients are extracted only after fixed-target
trivialization.

Equivalently, expand the fixed-target \(z\)-polynomial in the moving midpoint
parameter:
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
=
\sum_{j=0}^{7}(h-m)^j\mathcal B_j(z)
+
O((h-m)^8),
\qquad
\mathcal B_j(z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Although the coefficients still depend on the moving parameter \(h\), the
representatives themselves have already been transported to the fixed midpoint
fiber \(\mathfrak f_m\).

When we write \(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\) below, we mean this normalized
fixed-target fixed-sector package representative
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\), not the full unprojected matrix
cross-effect.
\end{definition}
```

This version avoids the ambiguity around whether \(B_j\) is an \(\mathfrak f_m\)-vector or an \(\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}\)-polynomial. I used \(\mathcal B_j(z)\) for the moving \((h-m)^j\)-coefficients of the whole \(z\)-polynomial.

---

## Edit 2 — Refine `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor`

**Replace the current definition with this.**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Package-level order-\(7\) quotient extractor]
\label{def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor}
For the normalized fixed-target package representative, define
\[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
[z^7]\,B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)
\in \mathfrak f_m .
\]
The quotient-valued order-\(7\) extractor is
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
\left[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\right]
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
\end{definition}
```

This now explicitly extracts \([z^7]\) from the fixed-target \(z\)-polynomial.

---

## Edit 3 — Refine `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients`

**Replace the current definition with this.**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Lower moving package coefficients]
\label{def:lower-moving-package-coefficients}
Let \(M\) be a residual-source monomial.  Define
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
\]
to be the coefficient of \(M\) in the fixed-target polynomial
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\).

For
\[
M_A:=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C:=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4,
\]
write
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
=
\sum_{j\ge0}(h-m)^j\mathcal B_j[M](z),
\qquad
\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
The lower moving representatives entering the order-\(7\) quotient-line
connection are the ordinary order-\(7\) coefficients
\[
B_j[M]:=[z^7]\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m,
\qquad
0\le j\le6.
\]

If
\[
B_j[M]=u_j^B[M]I+v_j^B[M]S
\]
and
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=\sum_{i\ge0}(h-m)^iA_i,
\qquad
A_i=u_i^AI+v_i^AS,
\]
then the order-\(7\) quotient-line connection correction on \(M\) is
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
In coordinates,
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\left(
u_{7-i}^B[M]v_i^A
-
u_i^Av_{7-i}^B[M]
\right).
\]
If \(A_i,B_j[M]\) are derivative-normalized rather than coefficient-normalized,
replace this formula by
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\binom{7}{i}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
\end{definition}
```

This resolves the “two order 7” issue cleanly:

- \([z^7]\) is ordinary \(z\)-order.
- \(B_j[M]\) is the \(j\)-th moving \((h-m)\)-coefficient **after** extracting the ordinary \(z^7\) coefficient.

---

## Edit 4 — Replace `rem:two-coefficient-indices-package`

Because the lower coefficient definition is now more precise, update the two-index remark accordingly.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Two coefficient indices]
\label{rem:two-coefficient-indices-package}
The subscript \(7\) in \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) denotes the ordinary \(z\)-order
coefficient extracted from the fixed-target package polynomial
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\).  By contrast, the index \(j\) in
\(B_j[M]\) denotes the coefficient of \((h-m)^j\) in the moving midpoint
expansion of a fixed residual monomial \(M\), after the ordinary \(z^7\)
coefficient has been extracted:
\[
B_j[M]=[z^7][(h-m)^jM]\,B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z).
\]
These are different indices.  The connection correction uses lower moving
coefficients \(B_{7-i}[M]\) to correct the quotient line at ordinary order
\(7\).
\end{remark}
```

---

## Edit 5 — Replace `rem:next-package-table-connection-correction`

This version now matches the refined \(z\)-polynomial definitions.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Next package table required for the connection correction]
\label{rem:next-package-table-connection-correction}
The next computable package table is not a raw Fréchet-family table.  It is the
moving lower-representative table
\[
A_i=[(h-m)^i]A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),
\qquad
1\le i\le7,
\]
together with
\[
B_j[M_A]=[z^7][(h-m)^jM_A]\,
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\]
and
\[
B_j[M_C]=[z^7][(h-m)^jM_C]\,
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\qquad
0\le j\le6,
\]
where
\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]
The connection-cancellation identities to prove are
\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]
and
\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]
These \(B_j[M]\) must be extracted from
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\), not from the raw seven-family
coefficient table.
\end{remark}
```

---

## Edit 6 — Replace `rem:representative-choices-lower-coefficient-tables`

The worker’s objection is correct: the prior independence statement was too strong termwise. Use this weaker, correct version.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Representative choices and lower coefficient tables]
\label{rem:representative-choices-lower-coefficient-tables}
The lower coefficients \(B_j[M]\) are representative-dependent.  The connection
expression is meaningful only after a single analytic representative and
midpoint trivialization convention have been fixed.  It is not invariant under
arbitrary termwise replacements
\[
B_j[M]\longmapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
\]
unless the direct term and the moving-line expansion are transformed
coherently as part of the same representative change.

Thus coefficient tables may choose any analytic section from
Definition~\ref{def:representative-level-midpoint-trivialization}, but they
must keep that section fixed through the final quotient-coordinate calculation.
Changing representatives in the middle of a finite table is not a valid
simplification.
\end{remark}
```

This is the main hardening. It prevents a false invariance claim.

---

No TeX insertion needed if the draft already contains:

```tex
...
\end{remark}
```

Keep it as a remark. Do **not** add the worker’s proposed `proof` environment unless you want to promote the parity lemma from audited WIP to final paper theorem. The short verification is valuable because \(M(-z)=M(z)^T\) is load-bearing for the \(Y\)-slot absence, but its current status as a verification sketch is the right tone.

## Net

Apply Edits **1–6**. Edit 7 is a “do nothing”: keep the verification sketch as a remark.

These changes make the package definitions internally coherent:

\[
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}
\to
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
\to
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
\to
[z^7]B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)
\to
Q_{7,m}^q.
\]

And they remove the only real technical overclaim left: termwise representative independence for the \(B_j[M]\)-connection table.

At [proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:2966), the draft says:

```tex
B_7^{\mathfrak f}
\bigl((1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\bigr)
\in
```

But later the draft correctly defines \(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\) as already source-bidegree-projected / non-\((1,1)\), amplitude-normalized, midpoint-trivialized. So applying \((1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}\) to it is stale.

Replace that display with:

```tex
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\in
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
```

```tex
If one keeps a full corrected package before source-bidegree projection, the
equivalent full-package formulation is obtained by applying
\((1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}\) before fixed-sector extraction and quotienting.
```

**2. Soften “Raw Cross-Effect Equivalent” Language**

At [proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:12746) and [proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:13074), the draft says the raw cross-effect formulation is “the same target” / “equivalent.” That is too strong unless the correction pipeline has already been applied.

Replace with:

```tex
At the raw cross-effect stage, the source-bidegree component feeding this
target is
\[
Q_{7,m}^q
\left(
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{raw}}
\right)=0.
\]
This becomes the UV-026 package statement only after edge divisibility,
amplitude normalization, midpoint trivialization, and fixed-sector extraction
have been performed.
```

This prevents agents from proving a raw statement and thinking they proved the package theorem.

**3. Fix The Source Primitive Support Table**

At [proof_of_rh.tex](C:/workspace/riemann2/rh/proof_of_rh.tex:28521), the text says same-point and mixed inputs both first appear at ordinary pre-whitening order \(a\). That conflicts with the Y-slot absence story: homogeneous grade-five mixed input has no ordinary \(z^5\) middle coefficient.

Replace the opening of that subsection with:

```tex
For a homogeneous scalar source grade
\[
\operatorname{Gr}_a r
=
\frac{r^{(a+2)}(m)}{(a+2)!}(t-m)^{a+2},
\]
the same-point input first appears at ordinary pre-whitening order \(a\).
The mixed input must also respect the transpose-parity table above, and need
not have a nonzero coefficient at ordinary order \(a\).
```

Then replace the grade table’s mixed column with something like:

```tex
1 & \delta G_\pm^{[1]}\text{ starts at }z^1
& \text{use the parity table for the corresponding mixed coefficient}
\\
5 & \delta G_\pm^{[5]}\text{ starts at }z^5
& [z^5]\mathfrak D_Q\delta N^{[5]}=0\text{ under the homogeneous convention}
```

That is the biggest mathematical hygiene fix from this pass. Everything else looks aligned with the current UV-026 workflow.

Final pass: I would not add another substantive section. The current draft already has the two necessary layers: corrected package definitions and finite Fréchet/Sylvester coefficient contracts. The remaining edits are small consistency/guardrail insertions.

I would add three patches.

---

## 1. Add a good-patch quotient-line convention remark

**Placement:** immediately after `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor`.

This makes explicit that determinant coordinates are valid only on the (A_5^{\mathfrak f}\neq0) patch. It also prevents future agents from treating (A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0) numerical tests as meaningful quotient tests. This matches the current working rule that determinant pairing is a good-patch coordinate, not the quotient definition itself. 

```tex
\begin{remark}[Good-patch convention for the quotient coordinate]
\label{rem:good-patch-convention-quotient-coordinate}
The quotient
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]
is the invariant object.  On a good patch where
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S\ne0,
\]
the determinant coordinate
\[
\mathcal D_m(Z):=(v_5\pi_I-u_5\pi_S)\pi_{\mathfrak f_m}(Z)
\]
identifies this one-dimensional quotient with \(\mathbf C\).  This coordinate
is not available on the rank-jump locus
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0.
\]
Consequently, baseline tests for which \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0\) are
quotient-inconclusive, not evidence for or against the quotient-gauge theorem.
\end{remark}
```

---

## 2. Add the homogeneous scalar-grade theorem as an explicit theorem obligation

**Placement:** inside `rem:definition-level-material-and-theorem-obligations`, after the existing “Source-weight-linear package input” item.

You already have `rem:status-homogeneous-grade-lemmas`, but the theorem-obligation list should explicitly say that the homogeneous scalar-grade convention is not merely notation; it is a proof obligation. This avoids treating the (Y)-slot absence theorem as final rather than conditional on the source-grade normalization.

```tex
\item \textbf{Homogeneous scalar-grade source theorem.}
One must prove that the residual scalar source convention
\[
\operatorname{Gr}_a r
=
\frac{r^{(a+2)}(m)}{(a+2)!}(t-m)^{a+2}
\]
is the correct source-grade decomposition for the actual corrected package.
In particular, the absence of the \(Y\)-slot families at
\(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) relies on this homogeneous scalar-grade convention.
If a different convention defines \(M^{[5]}\) as an ordinary matrix-output
order-\(5\) projection, then the \(Y\)-slot families
\[
L_1YR_1,\qquad L_2YR_0,\qquad L_0YR_2
\]
must be reopened and audited separately, as noted in
Remark~\ref{rem:quarantined-matrix-output-M5-convention}.
```

---

## 3. Fix a minor wording issue in the finite coefficient section

**Placement:** at the opening paragraph of `sec:finite-frechet-coefficient-calculus`.

The section says “This appendix” even though it is currently a `\section`, not necessarily in an appendix. Replace the first sentence with this neutral wording.

```tex
This section records the finite Fr\'echet/Sylvester coefficient recurrences
and finite gates used by the corrected-whitening package frontier of
Remark~\ref{rem:wip-first-cubic-source-transfer-target}.  These are table
contracts, not completed source tables.  Their role is to make the finite
computations auditable by agents and scripts without re-opening convention
choices already fixed in the main text.
```

---

## 4. Optional but useful: strengthen the raw-package failure remark by pointing to the quotient-line convention

**Placement:** append to `rem:computational-status-raw-package`.

This reinforces that the constant baseline result is inconclusive for structural reasons, while the nonconstant tests reject only the raw shortcut.

```tex
The word ``inconclusive'' for the constant baseline is meant in the precise
sense of Remark~\ref{rem:good-patch-convention-quotient-coordinate}: when
\(A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0\), the determinant coordinate does not define the
fixed-target quotient.  The nonconstant baseline failures occur on
nondegenerate good patches and therefore reject the raw Fr\'echet shortcut
under the tested convention.  They do not replace the corrected package
theorem, whose object is \(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\).
```

---

## Net result

No more large structural insertions are needed. The final cleanups are:

1. good-patch quotient coordinate guardrail;
2. homogeneous scalar-grade theorem obligation;
3. minor wording fix from “appendix” to “section”;
4. optional reinforcement in the raw-package failure remark.

Everything else now looks aligned with the latest frontier: raw Fréchet paths are quarantined, the corrected package object is defined conditionally, the finite coefficient calculus is a toolkit, and the next real missing data are the moving package tables (A_i) and (B_j[M]).

```tex
\[
```

```tex
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),

0\le j\le6,

where

+

R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}

\]

\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\), not from the raw seven-family
coefficient table.
\end{remark}
```

```tex
\begin{remark}[Representative choices and lower coefficient tables]
\label{rem:representative-choices-lower-coefficient-tables}

expression is meaningful only after a single analytic representative and
midpoint trivialization convention have been fixed.  It is not invariant under
arbitrary termwise replacements
\[
B_j[M]\longmapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
\]
unless the direct term and the moving-line expansion are transformed
coherently as part of the same representative change.

must keep that section fixed through the final quotient-coordinate calculation.
Changing representatives in the middle of a finite table is not a valid
simplification.
\end{remark}
```

This is the important correction: the earlier “independent of multiples of \(A_m\)” claim was too strong termwise.

## Edit 10 — Add good-patch quotient coordinate remark

**Placement:** immediately after `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Good-patch convention for the quotient coordinate]
\label{rem:good-patch-convention-quotient-coordinate}
The quotient
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]
is the invariant object.  On a good patch where
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S\ne0,
\]
the determinant coordinate
\[
\mathcal D_m(Z):=(v_5\pi_I-u_5\pi_S)\pi_{\mathfrak f_m}(Z)
\]
identifies this one-dimensional quotient with \(\mathbf C\).  This coordinate
is not available on the rank-jump locus
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0.
\]
Consequently, baseline tests for which \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0\) are
quotient-inconclusive, not evidence for or against the quotient-gauge theorem.
\end{remark}
```

This matches the current good-patch convention: determinant pairing is a coordinate only when \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\neq0\); the rank-jump locus needs exclusion or a separate convention.

```tex
\[

is the correct source-grade decomposition for the actual corrected package.
```

## User (2026-04-24 18:00:19)

## Claim

The quotient line used by the paper for (Q_{7,m}^q) and the determinant tests is the **canonical fixed midpoint line**
[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S.
]

No alternate denominator line is defined in the draft. The determinant is only the good-patch coordinate for this quotient, not the quotient definition.

## Status

Verified from the draft.

The paper first fixes the sector convention
[
\mathfrak f=\operatorname{span}{I,S},
\qquad
\mathfrak a=\operatorname{span}{D,J},
]
with
[
I=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\0&1\end{pmatrix},\quad
S=\begin{pmatrix}0&1\1&0\end{pmatrix},\quad
D=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\0&-1\end{pmatrix},\quad
J=\begin{pmatrix}0&-1\1&0\end{pmatrix}.
]
Then `def:fixed-sector-quintic-septic` defines
[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_5(h)),
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_7(h)),
]
and writes
[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S,
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_7(h)I+v_7(h)S.
]
The draft then states that the canonical order-(7) fixed-sector content is
[
[A_7^{\mathfrak f}]\in \mathfrak f/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
]
equivalently (\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7). 

## Canonical line

For the package-level object, `def:representative-level-midpoint-trivialization` explicitly sets
[
A(h):=A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)\in\mathfrak f_h,
\qquad
A_m:=A(m)=A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
]
then transports representatives into the fixed midpoint fiber (\mathfrak f_m). The normalized package representative is
[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
\in \mathfrak f_m[z]*{\le 7},
]
and `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor` defines
[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C*{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
[z^7],B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)
\in\mathfrak f_m,
]
with
[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
\bigl[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\bigr]
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]
So yes: (Q_{7,m}^q(C)) is valued in
[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]

## Good-patch caveat

The midpoint trivialization is explicitly conditional on the good patch
[
A(m)\ne0,
\qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\ne0.
]

At (A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0), the quotient line has rank jump: the denominator line is not a line. The draft does **not** give a canonical extension across that locus. This matches the computational-status remark: the constant baseline is called “quotient-inconclusive because (A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0),” while nonconstant baselines give nonzero determinant residuals. The right reading is: determinant-coordinate tests are valid on the good patch, but the rank-jump locus requires exclusion or a separate convention.

## Determinant functional

In the (I,S)-fixed sector, write
[
C=uI+vS,
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S.
]

The good-patch determinant coordinate is
[
\omega_m(C,A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m))
================================

uv_5-u_5v.
]

Thus the test for (C) to vanish in the quotient is
[
\boxed{uv_5-u_5v=0.}
]

For (C=A_7^{\mathfrak f}=u_7I+v_7S), this recovers
[
\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7.
]

But this scalarization is only a coordinate/test on the good patch. The quotient object remains
[
[C]\in \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]

## Honest verdict

Quotient-line mismatch is **not** a live explanation under the paper’s current definitions.

The denominator line is consistently
[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]
The prior raw numerical (A_5^{\mathfrak f}) line is part of the paper’s canonical denominator **only if** it is exactly this midpoint-trivialized fixed-sector quintic line. Otherwise it is merely a diagnostic/script convention. The draft itself quarantines the raw five-/seven-family tests as rejecting the **raw UV-026 shortcut**, not the corrected package theorem.

The main unresolved issue is therefore not the denominator line. It is the numerator/package representative:
[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr},
\qquad
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\qquad
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}),
]
including source-bidegree projection, amplitude normalization, midpoint trivialization, and the lower-coefficient connection correction. The draft also explicitly excludes determinant scalarization as a proof route by itself and requires any positive route to work before (\Phi_K), before determinant scalarization, and inside the fixed-target quotient package.

## Assistant (2026-04-24 18:00:28)

_Thought for 8s_

Here is the consolidated check. The suggestions are mostly correct, but I would **not** add all of them blindly. Some are already covered in the current draft, and some need softer wording. The remaining useful edits are below.

---

## Edit 1 — Fix coefficientwise trivialization

The worker is right: after

\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le 7}]
\]

the object is a finite \(z\)-polynomial with coefficients in \(\mathfrak f_h\). The midpoint trivialization should be applied coefficientwise.

**Replace `def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative` with:**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Normalized corrected-whitening fixed-sector package representative]
\label{def:normalized-corrected-whitening-package-representative}
Assume the amplitude-normalized package
\[
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}
\]
extends holomorphically to \(\delta=0\) through ordinary order \(7\).

First extract the moving fixed-sector \(z\)-polynomial
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
:=
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}(h)
\in
\mathfrak f_h[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Write
\[
Z_{\mathcal W}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}z^nZ_n(h),
\qquad
Z_n(h)\in\mathfrak f_h .
\]
The midpoint trivialization is applied coefficientwise:
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
:=
\sum_{n=0}^{7}
z^n\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}Z_n(h)
\in
\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Thus ordinary \(z\)-coefficients are extracted only after fixed-target
trivialization.

Equivalently, expand the fixed-target \(z\)-polynomial in the moving midpoint
parameter:
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
=
\sum_{j=0}^{7}(h-m)^j\mathcal B_j(z)
+
O((h-m)^8),
\qquad
\mathcal B_j(z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
Although the coefficients still depend on the moving parameter \(h\), the
representatives themselves have already been transported to the fixed midpoint
fiber \(\mathfrak f_m\).

When we write \(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}\) below, we mean this normalized
fixed-target fixed-sector package representative
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\), not the full unprojected matrix
cross-effect.
\end{definition}
```

---

## Edit 2 — Fix the quotient extractor accordingly

**Replace `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor` with:**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Package-level order-\(7\) quotient extractor]
\label{def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor}
For the normalized fixed-target package representative, define
\[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
[z^7]\,B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)
\in \mathfrak f_m .
\]
The quotient-valued order-\(7\) extractor is
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
\left[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\right]
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
\end{definition}
```

---

## Edit 3 — Fix lower moving coefficient definitions

**Replace `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients` with:**

```tex
\begin{definition}[Lower moving package coefficients]
\label{def:lower-moving-package-coefficients}
Let \(M\) be a residual-source monomial.  Define
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
\]
to be the coefficient of \(M\) in the fixed-target polynomial
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\).

For
\[
M_A:=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C:=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4,
\]
write
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
=
\sum_{j\ge0}(h-m)^j\mathcal B_j[M](z),
\qquad
\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
The lower moving representatives entering the order-\(7\) quotient-line
connection are the ordinary order-\(7\) coefficients
\[
B_j[M]:=[z^7]\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m,
\qquad
0\le j\le6.
\]

If
\[
B_j[M]=u_j^B[M]I+v_j^B[M]S
\]
and
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=\sum_{i\ge0}(h-m)^iA_i,
\qquad
A_i=u_i^AI+v_i^AS,
\]
then the order-\(7\) quotient-line connection correction on \(M\) is
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
In coordinates,
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\left(
u_{7-i}^B[M]v_i^A
-
u_i^Av_{7-i}^B[M]
\right).
\]
If \(A_i,B_j[M]\) are derivative-normalized rather than coefficient-normalized,
replace this formula by
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\binom{7}{i}
\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
\end{definition}
```

---

## Edit 4 — Replace the two-index remark

**Replace `rem:two-coefficient-indices-package` with:**

```tex
\begin{remark}[Two coefficient indices]
\label{rem:two-coefficient-indices-package}
The subscript \(7\) in \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) denotes the ordinary \(z\)-order
coefficient extracted from the fixed-target package polynomial
\(B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)\).  By contrast, the index \(j\) in
\(B_j[M]\) denotes the coefficient of \((h-m)^j\) in the moving midpoint
expansion of a fixed residual monomial \(M\), after the ordinary \(z^7\)
coefficient has been extracted:
\[
B_j[M]=[z^7][(h-m)^jM]\,B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z).
\]
These are different indices.  The connection correction uses lower moving
coefficients \(B_{7-i}[M]\) to correct the quotient line at ordinary order
\(7\).
\end{remark}
```

```tex
coherently as part of the same representative change.

simplification.
```

```tex
\[
\mathcal D_m(Z):=(v_5\pi_I-u_5\pi_S)\pi_{\mathfrak f_m}(Z)
\]
identifies this one-dimensional quotient with \(\mathbf C\).  This coordinate
is not available on the rank-jump locus
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0.
\]
Consequently, baseline tests for which \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0\) are
quotient-inconclusive, not evidence for or against the quotient-gauge theorem.
\end{remark}
```

This matches the quotient-line audit result: denominator-line mismatch is not the live issue; the quotient line is canonical, and determinant tests are good-patch coordinates only.

```tex
\item \textbf{Homogeneous scalar-grade source theorem.}
One must prove that the residual scalar source convention
\[
\operatorname{Gr}_a r
=
\frac{r^{(a+2)}(m)}{(a+2)!}(t-m)^{a+2}
\]
is the correct source-grade decomposition for the actual corrected package.

\(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\) relies on this homogeneous scalar-grade convention.
If a different convention defines \(M^{[5]}\) as an ordinary matrix-output
order-\(5\) projection, then the \(Y\)-slot families
\[
L_1YR_1,\qquad L_2YR_0,\qquad L_0YR_2
\]
must be reopened and audited separately, as noted in
Remark~\ref{rem:quarantined-matrix-output-M5-convention}.
```

## Claim

The quotient line used by the paper for (Q_{7,m}^q) and the determinant tests is the **canonical fixed midpoint line**
[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S.
]

No alternate denominator line is defined in the draft. The determinant is only the good-patch coordinate for this quotient, not the quotient definition.

## Status

Verified from the draft.

The paper first fixes the sector convention
[
\mathfrak f=\operatorname{span}{I,S},
\qquad
\mathfrak a=\operatorname{span}{D,J},
]
with
[
I=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\0&1\end{pmatrix},\quad
S=\begin{pmatrix}0&1\1&0\end{pmatrix},\quad
D=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\0&-1\end{pmatrix},\quad
J=\begin{pmatrix}0&-1\1&0\end{pmatrix}.
]
Then `def:fixed-sector-quintic-septic` defines
[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_5(h)),
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_7(h)),
]
and writes
[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S,
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_7(h)I+v_7(h)S.
]
The draft then states that the canonical order-(7) fixed-sector content is
[
[A_7^{\mathfrak f}]\in \mathfrak f/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
]
equivalently (\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7). 

## Canonical line

For the package-level object, `def:representative-level-midpoint-trivialization` explicitly sets
[
A(h):=A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)\in\mathfrak f_h,
\qquad
A_m:=A(m)=A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
]
then transports representatives into the fixed midpoint fiber (\mathfrak f_m). The normalized package representative is
[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
\in \mathfrak f_m[z]*{\le 7},
]
and `def:package-level-order-7-quotient-extractor` defines
[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C*{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
[z^7],B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(m;z)
\in\mathfrak f_m,
]
with
[
Q_{7,m}^q(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
:=
\bigl[
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr})
\bigr]
\in
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]
So yes: (Q_{7,m}^q(C)) is valued in
[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]

## Good-patch caveat

The midpoint trivialization is explicitly conditional on the good patch
[
A(m)\ne0,
\qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\ne0.
]

At (A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0), the quotient line has rank jump: the denominator line is not a line. The draft does **not** give a canonical extension across that locus. This matches the computational-status remark: the constant baseline is called “quotient-inconclusive because (A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0),” while nonconstant baselines give nonzero determinant residuals. The right reading is: determinant-coordinate tests are valid on the good patch, but the rank-jump locus requires exclusion or a separate convention.

## Determinant functional

In the (I,S)-fixed sector, write
[
C=uI+vS,
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S.
]

The good-patch determinant coordinate is
[
\omega_m(C,A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m))
================================

uv_5-u_5v.
]

Thus the test for (C) to vanish in the quotient is
[
\boxed{uv_5-u_5v=0.}
]

For (C=A_7^{\mathfrak f}=u_7I+v_7S), this recovers
[
\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7.
]

But this scalarization is only a coordinate/test on the good patch. The quotient object remains
[
[C]\in \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]

## Honest verdict

Quotient-line mismatch is **not** a live explanation under the paper’s current definitions.

The denominator line is consistently
[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
]
The prior raw numerical (A_5^{\mathfrak f}) line is part of the paper’s canonical denominator **only if** it is exactly this midpoint-trivialized fixed-sector quintic line. Otherwise it is merely a diagnostic/script convention. The draft itself quarantines the raw five-/seven-family tests as rejecting the **raw UV-026 shortcut**, not the corrected package theorem.

The main unresolved issue is therefore not the denominator line. It is the numerator/package representative:
[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr},
\qquad
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\qquad
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}),
]
including source-bidegree projection, amplitude normalization, midpoint trivialization, and the lower-coefficient connection correction. The draft also explicitly excludes determinant scalarization as a proof route by itself and requires any positive route to work before (\Phi_K), before determinant scalarization, and inside the fixed-target quotient package.

## 1. Quotient-line audit: accepted

I agree with the claim you pasted.

The canonical line is

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S.
\]

The good-patch determinant test is

\[
C=uI+vS,\qquad A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
\]

\[
\boxed{uv_5-u_5v=0.}
\]

The key point is that determinant scalarization is not the quotient definition. It is a coordinate on

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]

only when

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\neq0.
\]

So the earlier constant-baseline tests with \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0\) are correctly classified as quotient-inconclusive, not positive or negative evidence. The denominator line is not where the current failure sits.

---

## 2. Connection correction: formally correct, not yet computable

Agent 1’s conclusion is right.

The paper now defines the formal path:

\[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
\to
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
\to
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\amp}
\to
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z)
\to
B_j[M].
\]

But the actual lower package table

\[
B_j[M_A],\quad B_j[M_C],\qquad 0\le j\le6
\]

is not evaluated. The raw seven-family Fréchet rows cannot be substituted for it; that is exactly the shortcut already rejected. 

The useful mathematical reduction is:

\[
R_7[M]+\sum_{i=1}^7 \omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i)
=
[\eta^7]\omega_m(B(h;M),A_h^m),
\qquad \eta=h-m.
\]

So cancellation for \(M_A,M_C\) is equivalent to proving the \(\eta^7\)-coefficient of the moving package determinant vanishes in those channels.

That is a good reduction. It is not a proof.

---

## 3. Projection and amplitude audit: accepted, with one caveat

Agent 2’s projection audit is also right.

The projection

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}
\]

is correctly placed pre-\(\Phi_K\), pre-determinant, and pre-quotient. It retains mixed non-\((1,1)\) source bidegrees such as \((2,1)\) and \((1,2)\). Thus the residual monomials

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4
\]

are intended to survive if their actual tagged origin is \((2,1)\) or \((1,2)\). They are not removed definitionally. 

The caveat is important: the draft has not yet proved the source-bidegree compatibility theorem that certifies their exact tagged origin inside the actual corrected package. So the correct status is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{retained conditionally, not removed, but dependent on source-bidegree compatibility.}
}
\]

Also, amplitude normalization

\[
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2}
\]

does not mix source tags by itself. It can expose the edge coefficient after dividing by \(\delta^2\), but it cannot import discarded \((1,1)\) or pure one-source sectors after projection has already been applied. Any contamination would come from failure of edge divisibility or from losing source tags too early. 

---

## 4. Edge package / UV-022 / UV-010 audit: accepted

Agent 3’s status is correct and important.

The paper has:

\[
\boxed{\text{tagged pre-whitening source linearity}}
\]

but not yet:

\[
\boxed{\text{source-weight-linear corrected-whitening edge package}.}
\]

The promoted UV-025 block gives a valid source-level input, but it explicitly does not prove the corrected-whitening edge law, quotient package, \(B_7^{\mathfrak f}\), \(Q_{7,m}^q\), or UV-026. 

The missing theorem is essentially:

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
=
O(a_1\delta^2),
\]

plus holomorphic extension of

\[
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}.
\]

Without that, the normalized package is a well-defined conditional object, not an actual edge package. The target

\[
\overline E_{12}^{(7;1)}
=
\delta^2\mathcal H_7^q(m,\kappa,\delta^2)
\]

is still UV-010, not something already constructed. 

---

## 5. Live non-\(Y\) gate: accepted

Agent 5’s result is clean.

Under the homogeneous scalar-grade convention, the \(Y\)-slot families are not part of the active \(B_7\) gate. The first live non-\(Y\) gate is exactly

\[
L_2N_0R_1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
L_1N_0R_2.
\]

The live placements are:

\[
2+0+5=7,
\qquad
6+0+1=7.
\]

The four required vectors are:

\[
C_{112}^{L_2N_0R_1},
\quad
C_{122}^{L_2N_0R_1},
\quad
C_{112}^{L_1N_0R_2},
\quad
C_{122}^{L_1N_0R_2}.
\]

Each must land in \(\mathfrak f=\operatorname{span}\{I,S\}\) and satisfy

\[
uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]

But the draft only gives the table contract, not the populated table. The needed data are the leading first-Fréchet coefficients, leading second-Fréchet coefficients, \(N_0[0]\), and the relevant source/baseline jets. 

The reported formula for \(N_0[0]\),

\[
N_0[0]
=
\frac1\pi
\begin{pmatrix}
2q_0 & \frac12 q_0'\\[0.6ex]
\frac12 q_0' & \frac1{12}(q_0''+2q_0^3)
\end{pmatrix}_{t=m},
\]

is exactly the kind of concrete baseline input we need. But the draft still does not populate the full four-vector determinant test.

## What I would do next

There are two productive next attacks.

### Attack A — populate the live non-\(Y\) leading table

This is the most concrete finite computation:

\[
[z^2]\mathcal L_-^{\{1,1\}},
\quad
[z^6]\mathcal L_-^{\{1,5\}},
\quad
[z^2]\mathcal R_+^{\{1,1\}},
\quad
[z^6]\mathcal R_+^{\{1,5\}},
\]

\[
N_0[0],
\quad
[z^1]\Lambda_\pm^{[1]},
\quad
[z^5]\Lambda_\pm^{[5]}.
\]

Then compute the four \(C_{112}/C_{122}\) vectors and test

\[
uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]

This tells us whether the active homogeneous UV-026 gate actually closes.

### Attack B — construct the package-level lower moving table

This is more structural:

\[
A_i,\qquad B_j[M_A],\qquad B_j[M_C].
\]

Then test

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

This tells us whether the connection-corrected package cancels the raw residual.

## My recommendation

Do **Attack A first**.

It is narrower and less definition-dependent. It directly tests the current active homogeneous gate. If \(L_2N_0R_1/L_1N_0R_2\) fails, then the package theorem likely needs another correction layer. If it passes, confidence in the homogeneous-grade package route increases significantly.

Attack B remains necessary for the full corrected package theorem, but it depends on the higher package-layer construction and source-bidegree compatibility.

```text
5. Analyze representative dependence. State why arbitrary termwise changes
   \[
   B_j[M]\mapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
   \]
   are not valid unless the direct term and moving-line expansion transform coherently.
6. Reduce the connection test to a precise finite identity. In coordinates, this should be:
   \[
   R_7^{(A)}
   +
   \sum_{i=1}^{7}
   \left(
   u_{7-i}^B[M_A]v_i^A-u_i^Av_{7-i}^B[M_A]
   \right)=0,
   \]
   \[
   R_7^{(C)}
   +
   \sum_{i=1}^{7}
   \left(
   u_{7-i}^B[M_C]v_i^A-u_i^Av_{7-i}^B[M_C]
   \right)=0.
   \]
7. Decide whether this cancellation is proved, reduced to a table, or currently blocked.

Ground rules:
- Do not replace \(B_j[M]\) by raw seven-family coefficients.
- Do not use determinant scalarization as the quotient definition.
- Do not assume \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}=0\) cases are meaningful determinant tests; those are rank-jump / quotient-inconclusive unless the draft supplies a separate convention.
- Do not use downstream diagonal merger, state-locality, or \(\Phi_K\)-scalar invisibility.
- Work inside the fixed-target quotient package.

Output format:
Claim.
Status: proved / reduced / blocked / counterexample.
Typed definitions of \(A_i\), \(B_j[M]\), and \(\omega_m\).
Connection identity.
Representative-dependence analysis.
Minimal missing package theorem/table, if blocked.
Honest verdict.
```

_Thought for 9s_

Got it. Math-wise, the agent outputs are now consistent and useful. Here is the consolidated review.

## 1. Quotient-line audit

Accepted.

The denominator line is not a live problem. The paper consistently uses

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\qquad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)I+v_5(h)S.
\]

The determinant test

\[
C=uI+vS,\qquad A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
\]

\[
uv_5-u_5v=0
\]

is valid only as a good-patch coordinate when

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\neq0.
\]

So the correct conclusion is:

\[
\boxed{\text{quotient-line mismatch is not the main explanation.}}
\]

The problem is the numerator/package representative:

\[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr},
\qquad
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\qquad
B_7^{\mathfrak f}(C_{\mathcal W}^{\corr}).
\]

## 2. Connection-correction audit

Accepted, with a precise status.

The connection identity is well-typed:

\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]

For

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4,
\]

the required tests are

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

But these are not computable from the draft because the needed lower moving package table is not supplied:

\[
A_i,\qquad B_j[M_A],\qquad B_j[M_C].
\]

The important distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
B_j[M]\text{ must come from }B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\text{ not from raw seven-family coefficients.}
}
\]

So this branch is:

\[
\boxed{\text{reduced to package-level lower moving tables, not proved.}}
\]

The representative-dependence warning is also correct: arbitrary termwise replacements

\[
B_j[M]\mapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
\]

are not valid unless the direct term and moving-line expansion transform coherently.

## 3. Projection and amplitude audit

Accepted.

The projection is correctly placed:

\[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
\to
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
\to
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2}(\cdots)
\to
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\amp}.
\]

It is pre-\(\Phi_K\), pre-determinant, and pre-quotient.

The monomials

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4
\]

are intended to be retained non-\((1,1)\) mixed source terms, assuming their tagged bidegrees are actually \((2,1)\) or \((1,2)\). The draft does not yet prove that source-bidegree compatibility theorem.

Amplitude normalization

\[
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2}
\]

does not itself mix source bidegrees, since it carries no \(\tau_1,\tau_2\)-content. Any contamination would come from losing source tags too early or from failure of edge divisibility.

So status:

\[
\boxed{
\text{projection is correctly defined; source-bidegree compatibility remains open.}
}
\]

## 4. UV-022 / UV-010 edge-package audit

Accepted.

The key distinction is exactly right:

\[
\text{pre-whitening source linearity}
\neq
\text{source-weight-linear corrected-whitening edge package}.
\]

The paper has the former. It conditionally defines the latter. It does not yet prove the edge divisibility

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
=
O(a_1\delta^2),
\]

nor the holomorphic extension of

\[
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}.
\]

Thus the UV-010 edge law

\[
\overline E_{12}^{(7;1)}
=
\delta^2\mathcal H_7^q(m,\kappa,\delta^2)
\]

is still a target, not a constructed object.

Correct classification:

\[
\boxed{
\text{UV-025 is promoted; UV-022/023/024/026/010 remain open.}
}
\]

## 5. Live non-\(Y\) gate audit

Accepted. This is now the most concrete finite computation.

Under homogeneous scalar grade, the \(Y\)-slot families remain absent. The first live non-\(Y\) gate is

\[
L_2N_0R_1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
L_1N_0R_2.
\]

The order placements are correct:

\[
2+0+5=7,
\qquad
6+0+1=7.
\]

The four reduced vectors are:

\[
C_{112}^{L_2N_0R_1}
=
-\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
H_{-,2}^{1,1}\,N_0[0]\,\Lambda_{-,5}^{[5]}
\right),
\]

\[
C_{122}^{L_2N_0R_1}
=
-\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
H_{-,6}^{1,5}\,N_0[0]\,\Lambda_{-,1}^{[1]}
\right),
\]

\[
C_{112}^{L_1N_0R_2}
=
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
\Lambda_{-,1}^{[1]}\,N_0[0]\,H_{-,6}^{1,5}
\right),
\]

\[
C_{122}^{L_1N_0R_2}
=
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
\Lambda_{-,5}^{[5]}\,N_0[0]\,H_{-,2}^{1,1}
\right).
\]

The signs follow from midpoint parity:

\[
\Lambda_{+,1}^{[1]}=-\Lambda_{-,1}^{[1]},
\qquad
\Lambda_{+,5}^{[5]}=-\Lambda_{-,5}^{[5]},
\]

while

\[
H_{+,2}^{1,1}=H_{-,2}^{1,1},
\qquad
H_{+,6}^{1,5}=H_{-,6}^{1,5}.
\]

Each vector must satisfy

\[
C=uI+vS,
\qquad
uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]

The missing table is now minimal:

\[
\boxed{
\Lambda_{-,1}^{[1]},\quad
\Lambda_{-,5}^{[5]},\quad
H_{-,2}^{1,1},\quad
H_{-,6}^{1,5},\quad
N_0[0],\quad
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
}
\]

This is the clearest next finite gate.

## Consolidated current frontier

The agents narrowed the problem into two parallel gates:

### Gate A: finite non-\(Y\) coefficient gate

Compute

\[
\Lambda_{-,1}^{[1]},
\quad
\Lambda_{-,5}^{[5]},
\quad
H_{-,2}^{1,1},
\quad
H_{-,6}^{1,5},
\quad
N_0[0],
\]

then test the four fixed-sector vectors against \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\).

This is the most concrete next attack.

### Gate B: corrected package connection gate

Construct

\[
A_i,\qquad B_j[M_A],\qquad B_j[M_C],
\]

from the actual normalized corrected package

\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z),
\]

then test

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

This is structurally deeper and depends on edge divisibility/source-bidegree compatibility.

## My recommendation

Do Gate A first.

It is finite, local, and the missing table is small. If the four \(L_2N_0R_1/L_1N_0R_2\) vectors fail the determinant test, then the homogeneous UV-026 route has a serious obstruction. If they pass for structural reasons, it materially strengthens the corrected-package strategy.

Gate B should proceed in parallel only if you have an agent focused on package construction rather than coefficient algebra. Its blocker is not merely “more computation”; it is the actual lower moving package representative.

_Thought for 6s_

These two gate reports are useful. They shift the next step from broad “package theorem” language to two precise work items.

## Gate A — finite non-\(Y\) coefficient gate

I agree with the reduction. Under the homogeneous scalar-grade convention, the first live non-\(Y\) gate is

\[
L_2N_0R_1,\qquad L_1N_0R_2,
\]

with placements

\[
2+0+5=7,\qquad 6+0+1=7.
\]

The reduced four vectors are:

\[
C_{112}^{L_2N_0R_1}
=
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
(2WAWAW-WT_{2}^{1,1}W)NWBW
\right),
\]

\[
C_{122}^{L_2N_0R_1}
=
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
(WBWAW+WAWBW-WT_{6}^{1,5}W)NWAW
\right),
\]

\[
C_{112}^{L_1N_0R_2}
=
-\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
WAWN(WBWAW+WAWBW-WT_{6}^{1,5}W)
\right),
\]

\[
C_{122}^{L_1N_0R_2}
=
-\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\left(
WBWN(2WAWAW-WT_{2}^{1,1}W)
\right).
\]

where

\[
A=S_{-,1}^{[1]},\qquad B=S_{-,5}^{[5]},
\]

\[
T_2^{1,1}=\Sigma_-(-2AA),
\qquad
T_6^{1,5}=\Sigma_-( -(AB+BA)).
\]

So Gate A is now reduced to the small table:

\[
\boxed{
W_{-,0},\quad
\Sigma_-,\quad
X_{-,1}^{[1]},\quad
X_{-,5}^{[5]},\quad
N_0[0],\quad
u_5,v_5.
}
\]

That is a real simplification. It means the next finite-coefficient agent should not recompute all Stage 1 tables. It should target exactly these objects and then run the four determinant tests

\[
uv_5-u_5v=0.
\]

## Gate B — corrected package connection gate

I also agree with the Gate B classification:

\[
\boxed{\text{blocked on lower moving package table, not raw Fréchet coefficients.}}
\]

The well-typed identity is

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(B_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

But the draft does not yet compute

\[
B_j[M_A],\quad B_j[M_C],\qquad 0\le j\le6.
\]

The important warning is correct: arbitrary termwise changes

\[
B_j[M]\mapsto B_j[M]+c_jA_m
\]

are not valid simplifications, because the connection expression changes by

\[
\sum_{i=1}^{7}c_{7-i}\omega_m(A_m,A_i),
\]

which is not generally zero. The full moving representative and moving line must transform coherently.

So Gate B is not “more raw coefficient work.” It requires the actual normalized package table after:

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix},
\qquad
-a_1^{-1}\delta^{-2},
\qquad
\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}.
\]

## Priority

I would prioritize Gate A now.

Gate A has a compact finite table:

\[
W_{-,0},\quad
\Sigma_-,\quad
X_{-,1}^{[1]},\quad
X_{-,5}^{[5]},\quad
N_0[0],\quad
u_5,v_5.
\]

Gate B is structurally important but remains blocked on edge divisibility / source-bidegree compatibility / package table construction. Gate A can produce an actual pass/fail signal faster.

_Thought for 13s_

This is a good result. I would accept the **aggregate Gate A closure**, with two caveats.

## Verdict

\[
\boxed{
L_2N_0R_1+L_1N_0R_2
\text{ closes at the leading homogeneous non-}Y\text{ gate.}
}
\]

The mechanism is not quotient-gauge against \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\). It is stronger:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fixed-sector aggregate cancellation before quotienting.}
}
\]

That is exactly the kind of structural cancellation we were hoping to see.

## What is solid

The key proportionality

\[
X_{-,5}^{[5]}=\lambda X_{-,1}^{[1]},
\qquad
\lambda=\frac{1}{1920Q^8},
\]

is plausible under the homogeneous scalar-grade convention because both grade-one and grade-five same-point sources enter through the same \((I-D)\) channel, with different powers of \(Q\). The derived consequence

\[
B=\lambda A,
\]

then gives

\[
T_6^{1,5}=\lambda T_2^{1,1},
\qquad
H_{-,6}^{1,5}=\lambda H_{-,2}^{1,1},
\]

and hence collapses the four vectors into two left/right transpose-paired vectors. 

The transpose cancellation is also sound:

\[
(HNM)^T=MNH,
\]

and since \(\pi_{\mathfrak f}\) keeps the \(I,S\) part, it is transpose-invariant:

\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(HNM)=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(MNH).
\]

Therefore:

\[
C_{112}^{L_2N_0R_1}+C_{112}^{L_1N_0R_2}=0,
\]

\[
C_{122}^{L_2N_0R_1}+C_{122}^{L_1N_0R_2}=0.
\]

So the aggregate \(L_2N_0R_1/L_1N_0R_2\) gate closes without needing

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S.
\]

## Caveats to preserve

First, this closes the **aggregate pair**, not the individual family-by-family gauge tests. If someone insists on testing

\[
L_2N_0R_1
\]

or

\[
L_1N_0R_2
\]

separately, then the determinant test against \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\) is still not performed. But family-by-family closure is stronger than the package theorem needs.

Second, the proof depends on the homogeneous scalar-grade convention. If a future matrix-output \(M^{[5]}\) or non-homogeneous convention is revived, this aggregate closure needs to be re-audited.

## Updated frontier

The homogeneous UV-026 status is now:

\[
L_1YR_1,\quad L_2YR_0,\quad L_0YR_2
\]

absent at \(B_7\);

\[
L_2N_0R_1+L_1N_0R_2
\]

closed by aggregate transpose cancellation;

so the next live homogeneous non-\(Y\) target is:

\[
\boxed{
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3.
}
\]

That should be attacked as an **aggregate package pair first**, not family-by-family gauge.

## Current mathematical read

This is the first genuinely encouraging UV-026 development in this lane. It shows the homogeneous convention is not merely avoiding the \(Y\)-slot problem; it also produces a real left/right structural cancellation in the first live non-\(Y\) gate.

The next all-left/all-right gate is now the decisive local test.

_Thought for 5s_

This is a strong result, but I would mark it **provisionally accepted pending one focused verification**.

## What looks correct

The aggregate cancellation mechanism is structurally the same as Gate A:

\[
\text{left contribution}=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(GNW),
\]

\[
\text{right contribution}=-\pi_{\mathfrak f}(WNG),
\]

and since

\[
(GNW)^T=WNG,
\]

with \(\pi_{\mathfrak f}\) transpose-invariant, the aggregate cancels:

\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(GNW)-\pi_{\mathfrak f}(WNG)=0.
\]

That is a valid fixed-sector cancellation mechanism, and it is stronger than quotient-gauge.

So the claimed conclusion is plausible:

\[
\boxed{
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3=0
\quad\text{in }\mathfrak f
}
\]

at the leading homogeneous \((1,1,5)\) gate. 

## The one thing I would verify

The only nontrivial step that should be independently checked is:

\[
G^+_{7;1,1,5}=(-1)^7G^-_{7;1,1,5}=-G.
\]

This depends on the midpoint parity convention for the **third-Fréchet coefficient table** after source tags and homogeneous grades are fixed. The draft apparently says the same parity convention applies to third-Fréchet coefficients once the \(G^\pm\)-table is defined, but because this is the load-bearing sign, I would have one verifier check it.

A second thing to check, but less concerning, is:

\[
U_{-,7}^{1,1,5}=\lambda U_{-,3}^{1,1,1},
\qquad
G^-_{7;1,1,5}=\lambda G^-_{3;1,1,1}.
\]

This proportionality is not actually needed for the left/right cancellation if the \(G\) used on both sides is the same \(G^-_{7;1,1,5}\). It helps explain structure, but the cancellation only needs:

\[
G^+_{7;1,1,5}=-G^-_{7;1,1,5},
\]

plus symmetry of \(G,N,W\).

## Updated UV-026 state

Assuming the parity check passes, the homogeneous leading cubic gates are now:

\[
L_1YR_1,\quad L_2YR_0,\quad L_0YR_2
\]

absent at \(B_7\);

\[
L_2N_0R_1+L_1N_0R_2
\]

closed by aggregate transpose cancellation;

\[
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3
\]

closed by aggregate transpose cancellation.

So the leading homogeneous \((1,1,5)\) cubic UV-026 gate is effectively closed **at aggregate package level**.

That does **not** yet prove the whole corrected package theorem, because Gate B remains:

\[
B_j[M_A],\quad B_j[M_C],
\]

edge divisibility, source-bidegree compatibility, and lower moving package tables. But it removes the raw homogeneous cubic obstruction.
