# 5. Replace `def:lower-moving-package-coefficients` if it still uses \(B_j\) in the connection formula

```tex
\begin{definition}[Lower moving package coefficients and connection data]
\label{def:lower-moving-package-coefficients}
Let \(M\) be a residual-source monomial and set \(\epsilon=h-m\).
After Gate~B raw edge-divisibility, define the pre-trivialization edge
coefficients by
\[
Z_j[M]
:=
-[z^7][\epsilon^j\delta^2M]\,
a_1^{-1}\tau_{h\to m}
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{raw}},
\qquad
0\le j\le 6.
\]
Equivalently,
\[
Z_j[M]
=
[z^7][\epsilon^jM]\,
\tau_{h\to m}\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{amp}}
\]
once the edge-normalized package has been formed.

By contrast, let
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
\]
be the coefficient of \(M\) in the fixed-target package representative after
representative-level midpoint trivialization, and write
\[
B_{\mathrm{pkg}}^{\mathfrak f}(h;z;M)
=
\sum_{j\ge0}\epsilon^j\mathcal B_j[M](z),
\qquad
\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
The post-trivialization lower representatives are
\[
B_j[M]:=[z^7]\mathcal B_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m.
\]
These coefficients are useful for recording the final representative, but they
must not be inserted into the moving-line connection formula, because the
trivialization has already transported along the moving quotient line.

Let
\[
A_h^m:=\tau_{h\to m}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),
\qquad
A_h^m=\sum_{i\ge0}\epsilon^iA_i,
\qquad
A_i=u_i^AI+v_i^AS\in\mathfrak f_m.
\]
If
\[
Z_j[M]=u_j^Z[M]I+v_j^Z[M]S,
\]
then the order-\(7\) quotient-line connection correction on \(M\) is
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
In coordinates,
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\left(
u_{7-i}^Z[M]v_i^A
-
u_i^Av_{7-i}^Z[M]
\right).
\]
If \(A_i,Z_j[M]\) are derivative-normalized rather than
coefficient-normalized, replace this formula by
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\binom{7}{i}
\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
\end{definition}
```

---

# 6. Replace `rem:next-package-table-connection-correction`

```tex
\begin{remark}[Next package table required for the connection correction]
\label{rem:next-package-table-connection-correction}
The next computable package table is not a raw Fréchet-family table and not
a post-trivialization representative table.  The connection correction requires
the transported moving-line table
\[
A_i=[\epsilon^i]\tau_{h\to m}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),
\qquad
1\le i\le7,
\qquad
\epsilon=h-m,
\]
together with the pre-trivialization edge coefficients
\[
Z_j[M_A]
=
-[z^7][\epsilon^j\delta^2M_A]\,
a_1^{-1}\tau_{h\to m}
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{raw}},
\]
and
\[
Z_j[M_C]
=
-[z^7][\epsilon^j\delta^2M_C]\,
a_1^{-1}\tau_{h\to m}
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\mathrm{mix}}C_{\mathcal W}^{\mathrm{raw}},
\qquad
0\le j\le6,
\]
where
\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]
The connection-cancellation identities to prove are
\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]
and
\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}
\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]
The post-trivialization coefficients \(B_j[M]\) may be recorded after applying
\(\operatorname{Triv}_{h\to m}\), but inserting them into the displayed
connection formula double-counts the moving quotient-line correction.
\end{remark}
```

# 7. Replace / shorten `rem:pre-post-trivialization-lower-coefficients`

If this remark exists separately and duplicates the full definition, replace it with:

```tex
\begin{remark}[Pre- and post-trivialization lower coefficients]
\label{rem:pre-post-trivialization-lower-coefficients}
Definition~\ref{def:lower-moving-package-coefficients} uses
pre-trivialization edge coefficients \(Z_j[M]\) in the connection formula
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]
Post-trivialization coefficients \(B_j[M]\) already include the moving-line
connection contribution.  Inserting \(B_j[M]\) into the displayed
pre-trivialization connection formula would double-count the moving-line
correction.
\end{remark}
```

```tex
and the pre-trivialization lower edge coefficients

for

The required cancellation identities are

R_7^{(A)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,

and
\[
R_7^{(C)}+\sum_{i=1}^7\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.

These are package-level pre-trivialization connection identities, not raw
seven-family identities and not post-trivialization \(B_j[M]\) identities.
```

# 10. Add `rem:dependency-ledger-gate-b`

Place immediately after `cor:gate-b-raw-edge-divisibility-order-seven`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Dependency ledger for Gate B]
\label{rem:dependency-ledger-gate-b}
Corollary~\ref{cor:gate-b-raw-edge-divisibility-order-seven} is a raw
edge-divisibility result, not a post-edge quotient compatibility result.
Its inputs are logically independent and should not be silently replaced by
quotient-level language.

The dependencies are:
\begin{enumerate}
\item Gate~A aggregate closure, which removes the leading homogeneous cubic
non-\((1,1)\) contribution.
\item All-subset midpoint parity for the same-point whitening coefficients,
which pairs labelled subsets with their complements.
\item Symmetry of the relevant same-point subset coefficients and of
\(N_0[0]\).
\item Transpose-invariance of the moving fixed-sector projection
\(\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}\).
\item Leading \(Y\)-middle parity, which removes odd \(Y\)-middle entries
and makes the even entries compatible with subset-complement cancellation.
\item Moving homogeneous source-frame telescoping, which cancels the
grade-lowering source-motion term against the same-basis derivative of the
adjacent lower grade.
\item \(2\)-jet source-frame normalization, which removes the affine lower
boundary produced by differentiating \(\operatorname{Gr}_0\).
\item Differentiation of the full Taylor source tower before ordinary-order
truncation, which prevents an artificial upper-boundary residual.
\end{enumerate}

If any one of these inputs is removed, the statement should be demoted back
to a conditional Gate~B target rather than used as a proved package
construction.  Thus the corollary should be cited as a conditional raw
edge-divisibility theorem: it closes Gate~B for the corrected package only
after the listed parity, source-frame, and full-tower-before-truncation
hypotheses have been identified with the actual source model.
\end{remark}
```

---

# 11. Add `rem:what-remains-attackable-in-gate-b`

Place after the dependency ledger.

```tex
\begin{remark}[What remains attackable in Gate B]
\label{rem:what-remains-attackable-in-gate-b}
After Corollary~\ref{cor:gate-b-raw-edge-divisibility-order-seven}, the
leading cubic inventory, the \(d\ge4\) amplitude terms, and the raw
source-motion lower-shadow terms should not be reopened as independent
obstructions unless one of the stated hypotheses is rejected.  A valid attack
on Gate~B must now target one of the hypotheses:
\[
\text{all-subset midpoint parity},\quad
\text{leading \(Y\)-middle parity},\quad
\text{moving source-frame telescoping},\quad
\text{affine boundary removal},\quad
\text{full-tower-before-truncation}.
\]
In particular, a nonzero unsymmetrized transfer placement is not a Gate~B
counterexample unless it survives the labelled aggregate and the source-frame
telescoping procedure.
\end{remark}
```

# 12. Add `rem:negative-findings-gate-a-gate-b-audit`

Place after `rem:raw-placement-level-vanishing-false`, or after the Gate B guardrails.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Negative findings from the Gate A/Gate B audit]
\label{rem:negative-findings-gate-a-gate-b-audit}
The Gate A/Gate B audit rules out several tempting shortcuts.

\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Placement-level vanishing is false.}
Individual transfer placements can have nonzero fixed-sector components.
The cancellation is only after labelled subset-complement aggregation and
transpose-invariant fixed-sector projection.

\item \textbf{Family-by-family gauge is too strong.}
The package target is aggregate fixed-sector cancellation or quotient
invisibility.  It does not require each Fréchet family to be separately
proportional to \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\).

\item \textbf{Post-edge quotient compatibility is too late.}
A statement such as
\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C)=Q_{7,m}^q(\Pi_{1,1}C)
\]
cannot be used to prove raw edge-divisibility unless it is reformulated at
the raw edge layer.

\item \textbf{Determinant scalarization is only a good-patch coordinate.}
The quotient object is
\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
The scalar determinant test is a coordinate on a nondegenerate patch, not the
definition of the quotient.

\item \textbf{The matrix-output \(M^{[5]}\) convention is quarantined.}
The active proof uses homogeneous scalar grades.  Replacing the grade-five
source by an ordinary matrix-output order-\(5\) coefficient reopens the
\(Y\)-slot families and requires a separate no-double-counting theorem against
grades \(0,2,4\).

\item \textbf{The \(Z_j\)-table is post-Gate-B, not the Gate-B obstruction.}
After raw edge-divisibility, \(Z_j[M]\) is the next pre-trivialization table
for the connection calculation.  It is not a missing proof of the
\(\delta^2\)-edge law itself.
\end{enumerate}
\end{remark}
```

---

# 13. Add `rem:mechanism-gate-b-cancellation`

Place after the negative findings.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Mechanism of the Gate B cancellation]
\label{rem:mechanism-gate-b-cancellation}
The Gate~B cancellation is not a brute-force finite coefficient accident.  It
is the combination of three structural symmetries:
\[
\text{odd total-grade subset-complement cancellation}
\]
for amplitude terms,
\[
\text{moving Taylor-frame telescoping}
\]
for source-motion lower terms, and
\[
\text{\(2\)-jet source-frame normalization}
\]
for the affine boundary.  These mechanisms act before \(\Phi_K\), before
determinant scalarization, and before quotient extraction.  The resulting raw
edge-divisibility is exactly the input needed to construct the normalized
order-\(7\) corrected package.
\end{remark}
```

```tex
\begin{remark}[Next object after Gate B]
\label{rem:next-object-after-gate-b}
After Gate~B, the next finite table is not a raw seven-family coefficient
table.  It is the pre-trivialization \(\delta^2\)-edge table
\[
Z_j[M]
=
-[\epsilon^j\delta^2M]\,
a_1^{-1}
\tau_{h\to m}\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}.
\]
This table is defined only after raw edge-divisibility makes the
\(\delta^{-2}\)-normalized package holomorphic.  The finite Frechet/Sylvester
calculus supplies the raw coefficient infrastructure for computing \(Z_j[M]\);
it does not replace the edge-divisibility theorem.
\end{remark}
```

# 21. Replace all-left/all-right appendix subsection

Replace `app:pending-all-left-all-right-cubic-table` with:

```tex
\subsection{All-left/all-right cubic aggregate table}
\label{app:all-left-all-right-cubic-aggregate-table}

The all-left and all-right families
\[
L_3N_0R_0,
\qquad
L_0N_0R_3
\]
are order-count admissible placements, but they are not live Gate~A residuals
under the third-Fréchet midpoint-parity convention.  Let
\[
G:=G^-_{7;1,1,5},
\qquad
W:=W_{-,0}=W_{+,0},
\qquad
N:=N_0[0].
\]
The two aggregate fixed-sector contributions are
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(GNW)
\]
and
\[
-\pi_{\mathfrak f}(WNG).
\]
Since \(G,N,W\) are symmetric,
\[
(GNW)^T=WNG,
\]
and since \(\pi_{\mathfrak f}\) is transpose-invariant,
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(GNW)=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(WNG).
\]
Therefore
\[
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3=0
\quad\text{in }\mathfrak f
\]
at the aggregate fixed-sector level, conditional on the third-Fréchet
midpoint-parity convention.

Thus the required table is not a pending quotient-gauge determinant table.
The remaining obligation is to verify that the third-Fréchet coefficient
normalization used by any coefficient agent is the same symmetric multilinear
normalization used in Lemma~\ref{lem:aggregate-closure-leading-all-left-all-right-cubic-gate}.
If an ordered-triple convention is used instead, the ordered-permutation
aggregate must be included consistently on both sides before applying the
transpose-pairing argument.
```

# Final scope

After these insertions, the paper should be internally consistent on the main points:

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate A closes in aggregate.}}
\]

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate B closes conditionally through ordinary order }7.}
\]

\[
\boxed{\text{\(Z_j[M]\), not \(B_j[M]\), enters the connection formula.}}
\]

\[
\boxed{\text{The finite coefficient appendix is diagnostic, not a list of live Gate A obligations.}}
\]

\[
\boxed{\text{The package-to-jet/global bridge remains downstream.}}
\]

## Net verdict

No large additions are needed. The draft now captures the major structural findings:

[
\text{Gate A} \neq \text{Gate B},
]

Gate A closes in aggregate, Gate B is raw edge-divisibility, raw placement-level vanishing is false, and post-edge quotient compatibility cannot be used to prove raw edge-divisibility. The only remaining changes are wording cleanups to prevent stale “live/pending” interpretations in the appendix and raw-shortcut remarks.

insert:

```tex
\begin{remark}[Final status of the Gate A/Gate B package layer]
\label{rem:final-status-gate-a-gate-b-package-layer}
At this stage the corrected-package construction has the following status.

Gate~A is closed at the aggregate fixed-sector level under the active
homogeneous scalar-grade convention and the stated third-Fr\'echet midpoint
parity convention.  It should not be reopened as a family-by-family
determinant-gauge problem.

Gate~B is closed only in the conditional raw edge-divisibility sense of
Corollary~\ref{cor:gate-b-raw-edge-divisibility-order-seven}: its use
depends on the stated midpoint-parity, source-frame telescoping,
\(2\)-jet source-frame normalization, and full-Taylor-tower-before-truncation
hypotheses.  If one of those hypotheses is not verified in the final source
model, Gate~B reverts to an open raw edge-divisibility target, not to a
post-edge quotient-compatibility statement.

After Gate~B, the next local package table is the pre-trivialization
\(Z_j[M]\)-table, and the next local identities are
\[
R_7^{(A)}+\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\qquad
R_7^{(C)}+\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]
The post-trivialization coefficients \(B_j[M]\) must not be substituted into
these connection sums.

Finally, none of Gate~A, Gate~B, or the \(Z_j[M]\)-connection package by
itself proves the global package-to-\(\Xi_\zeta^{(N)}\) hidden-state bridge
or the final finite-core contradiction.  Those remain downstream theorem
obligations.
\end{remark}
```

Reason: this consolidates the final proof-state in one place: Gate A closed, Gate B conditional, (Z_j) next, global bridge still downstream. The (Z_j) convention is especially important because the status notes warn that (B_j[M]) would double-count the quotient-line connection.

* Gate B raw-layer edge divisibility before quotient compatibility.

* Package-to-transform bridge still downstream.

## 2. Replace `cor:amplitude-closure-higher-transfers`

This removes undefined summary names \(V_4\) and \(R_{0115}\) while preserving the result.

```tex
\begin{corollary}[Amplitude closure of higher transfers]
\label{cor:amplitude-closure-higher-transfers}
The amplitude part of every Gate~B transfer of total homogeneous grade \(7\)
vanishes in \(\mathfrak f_h\).  In particular, the aggregate fourth-transfer
positive-grade contribution
\[
\sum_{J\in\{(1,1,1,4),(1,1,2,3),(1,2,2,2)\}}
[z^7]\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}\mathcal T_4(J)
\]
vanishes.

The same argument also kills the first lower-shadow fourth-transfer aggregate
\[
[z^7]\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}\mathcal T_4(0,1,1,5)=0.
\]
More generally, the subset-complement argument applies to every labelled
multiset of total homogeneous grade \(7\), including zero-padded
lower-shadow multisets, provided the full Taylor source tower is
differentiated before ordinary-order truncation.

Thus the amplitude parts also vanish for
\[
d=5:\quad (1,1,1,1,3),\ (1,1,1,2,2),
\]
\[
d=6:\quad (1,1,1,1,1,2),
\]
and
\[
d=7:\quad (1,1,1,1,1,1,1).
\]
\end{corollary}
```

---

## 3. Add polynomial/vector notation clarification for \(Z_j[M]\)

**Placement:** immediately after `def:pre-trivialization-edge-table-after-gate-b`.

```tex
\begin{remark}[Polynomial versus vector notation for \(Z_j[M]\)]
\label{rem:polynomial-versus-vector-notation-zj}
Definition~\ref{def:pre-trivialization-edge-table-after-gate-b} first defines
the pre-trivialization edge table as a polynomial
\[
Z_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}.
\]
When \(Z_j[M]\) appears without the argument \(z\) in the moving-line
connection formula, it denotes its ordinary \(z^7\)-coefficient:
\[
Z_j[M]:=[z^7]Z_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m.
\]
Thus
\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i)
\]
uses the vector coefficient \([z^7]Z_{7-i}[M](z)\), not the full polynomial.
\end{remark}
```

---

## Assistant (2026-04-25 06:17:09)

_Thought for 50s_

We are now in a much better position than a few days ago, but **not at a complete RH proof**.

The current state is best described as:

\[
\boxed{
\text{the local corrected-package construction is now largely organized,}
}
\]

but

\[
\boxed{
\text{the global package-to-transform / finite-core endgame remains open.}
}
\]

## 1. What is now essentially settled

### Gate A is closed

Gate A is the leading homogeneous cubic non-\((1,1)\) audit. It is no longer a live obstruction.

The accepted status is:

\[
F_1^{\mathrm{lead\ cubic}}=0.
\]

Mechanism:

\[
L_1YR_1,\quad L_2YR_0,\quad L_0YR_2
\]

are absent under homogeneous scalar grade, while

\[
L_2N_0R_1+L_1N_0R_2=0
\]

and, assuming third-Fréchet midpoint parity,

\[
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3=0
\]

close in aggregate in \(\mathfrak f\). This is **aggregate fixed-sector cancellation**, not family-by-family determinant-gauge. 

### Gate B is conditionally closed through ordinary order \(7\)

Gate B is the raw edge-divisibility theorem:

\[
[z^7][\delta^1]\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}=0.
\]

The current proof mechanism is:

\[
\text{Gate A aggregate closure}
+
\text{odd total-grade subset-complement cancellation}
+
\text{moving source-frame telescoping}
+
\text{\(2\)-jet affine-boundary removal}.
\]

So the previous \(d\ge4\) transfer / lower-shadow obstruction is now superseded by a structural cancellation, not by brute-force enumeration. The raw edge law is conditional on the stated source-frame/parity hypotheses, especially all-subset midpoint parity, leading \(Y\)-middle parity, moving homogeneous source-frame telescoping, affine boundary removal, and differentiating the full Taylor tower before truncation. turn181file9

### The \(Z_j[M]\) convention is now the correct post-Gate-B object

After Gate B, the next local table is not another raw seven-family audit. It is the pre-trivialization edge table:

\[
Z_j[M].
\]

The connection formula must use

\[
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i),
\]

not post-trivialization \(B_j[M]\), because \(B_j[M]\) already includes the moving-line correction. This is now one of the core bookkeeping guardrails. turn181file13

## 2. What remains locally

The next local package work is:

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0,
\]

where

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]

This is now **downstream of Gate B**, not part of proving raw edge-divisibility. So the local frontier has moved from “Can we make the package holomorphic?” to “Can we compute the pre-trivialization \(Z_j\)-table and close the connection identities?”

## 3. What remains globally

Even if the \(Z_j\)-connection package closes, that is still not the full proof.

The remaining large obligations are:

1. **Package-to-jet / hidden-state bridge.**  
   Show that the corrected package actually feeds the \(\Xi_\zeta^{(N)}\) hidden-state / first surviving odd coefficient in the required way.

2. **Diagonal merger / \(\kappa\)-independence / descent.**  
   The local quotient package must descend cleanly through the actual transform geometry, not merely through the formal finite package.

3. **Finite-core branch.**  
   The current asymptotic contradiction is pair-like. The general finite-core case remains a separate branch if the first odd coefficient cancels but a higher odd anomaly survives.

4. **Effective threshold and low-height bridge.**  
   The endgame currently excludes sufficiently large pair-like anomalies. A complete RH proof would need an effective \(Q_*\), conversion to a height threshold \(T_*\), and finite low-height verification or another low-height argument. The draft’s earlier final theorem was correctly downgraded to an asymptotic pair-like branch exclusion. turn181file15

## 4. Strategic summary

The proof is no longer stuck at the old UV-026/Gate A/Gate B coefficient audit.

The current state is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate A closed.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate B conditionally closed through order }7.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{next local task: }Z_j[M_A],Z_j[M_C]\text{ and connection cancellation.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{next global task: package-to-jet / finite-core / effective endgame.}
}
\]

So the proof has advanced from “we may have a local package obstruction” to “the local package layer has a coherent conditional closure path, and the main unresolved work is now downstream.”

## Honest verdict

We are **not** at a final proof of RH.

But we are also no longer merely exploring random coefficient gates. The current proof architecture has a much sharper frontier:

\[
\boxed{
\text{finish the post-Gate-B connection package, then attack the package-to-transform bridge.}
}
\]

That is the next high-value direction.

## 11. Add reduced package vs hidden-state equality warning

```tex
\begin{remark}[Reduced package equality does not imply hidden-state equality]
\label{rem:reduced-package-equality-not-hidden-state-equality}
The reduced datum \(\widehat\Psi\) forgets the raw septic representative in
the affine line
\[
A_7^{\mathfrak f}\longmapsto A_7^{\mathfrak f}+\chi A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]
This forgotten direction is not automatically invisible to the transverse
functional.  Writing
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}=u_5 I+v_5 S,
\]
one has
\[
\Phi_K(A_5^{\mathfrak f})=2v_5.
\]
Thus reduced-package equality alone cannot imply agreement modulo
\(\ker\Phi_K\) through the first surviving odd order, except on the special
locus \(v_5=0\) or after an additional theorem showing cancellation in the
actual corrected block.  The missing hidden-state theorem is therefore a
transform-level state-locality statement modulo \(\ker\Phi_K\), not a formal
consequence of quotient-septic closure.
\end{remark}
```

## 13. Add Bottleneck C fiber-selection guardrail

```tex
\begin{remark}[Bottleneck C is exceptional-divisor fiber selection]
\label{rem:bottleneck-c-exceptional-divisor-fiber-selection}
For Bottleneck C, the obstruction is not the choice of a local septic
representative such as \(v_7=0\), \(u_7=0\), or \(T=v_7/c\).  Those are
diagnostic data for the hidden-state problem.  The C-side object has already
quotiented raw septic representatives away.  Its remaining obstruction is the
exceptional-divisor trace
\[
B(m,\kappa):=\widetilde\Psi^{\corr}_{\mathrm{red}}(m,\kappa,0).
\]
Analyticity, swap-evenness, fixed codomain, scalar normalization, and the
central value \(B(m,0)=\widehat\Psi(m)\) do not force
\[
\partial_\kappa B=0.
\]
A valid C proof must supply actual diagonal merger, same-reduced-image, or
collision-functoriality for the corrected two-atom package.
\end{remark}
```

## 14. Add formal countermodel warning for C

```tex
\begin{remark}[Formal C properties do not rule out slope leakage]
\label{rem:formal-c-properties-do-not-rule-out-slope-leakage}
Formal properties such as analyticity, swap-evenness, fixed codomain, scalar
normalization, and the central value at \(\kappa=0\) do not by themselves
force the C-side exceptional-divisor trace to be independent of \(\kappa\).
A formal model may have
\[
B_3=Z+\varepsilon\kappa,
\]
or, at the quotient-defect level,
\[
R\longmapsto R+R_\kappa,
\qquad
\det(R_\kappa,A_5^{\mathfrak f})=c^2\varepsilon\kappa.
\]
Thus representative-gauge invariance only kills additions parallel to
\(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\).  It does not kill determinant-changing quotient-defect
data.  A C proof must control the actual corrected exceptional-divisor
quotient class, not merely the formal shape of the reduced package.
\end{remark}
```

## 1. What is now essentially settled

### Gate A is closed

The accepted status is:

[

close in aggregate in (\mathfrak f). This is **aggregate fixed-sector cancellation**, not family-by-family determinant-gauge. 

### Gate B is conditionally closed through ordinary order (7)

Gate B is the raw edge-divisibility theorem:

[

[

+
\text{odd total-grade subset-complement cancellation}
+
\text{moving source-frame telescoping}
+
\text{(2)-jet affine-boundary removal}.
]

So the previous (d\ge4) transfer / lower-shadow obstruction is now superseded by a structural cancellation, not by brute-force enumeration. The raw edge law is conditional on the stated source-frame/parity hypotheses, especially all-subset midpoint parity, leading (Y)-middle parity, moving homogeneous source-frame telescoping, affine boundary removal, and differentiating the full Taylor tower before truncation.

### The (Z_j[M]) convention is now the correct post-Gate-B object

After Gate B, the next local table is not another raw seven-family audit. It is the pre-trivialization edge table:

[
Z_j[M].
]

The connection formula must use

[
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i),
]

not post-trivialization (B_j[M]), because (B_j[M]) already includes the moving-line correction. This is now one of the core bookkeeping guardrails.

## 2. What remains locally

The next local package work is:

[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
]

[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0,

where

[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad

This is now **downstream of Gate B**, not part of proving raw edge-divisibility. So the local frontier has moved from “Can we make the package holomorphic?” to “Can we compute the pre-trivialization (Z_j)-table and close the connection identities?”

## 3. What remains globally

Even if the (Z_j)-connection package closes, that is still not the full proof.

The remaining large obligations are:

1. **Package-to-jet / hidden-state bridge.**
   Show that the corrected package actually feeds the (\Xi_\zeta^{(N)}) hidden-state / first surviving odd coefficient in the required way.

2. **Diagonal merger / (\kappa)-independence / descent.**
   The local quotient package must descend cleanly through the actual transform geometry, not merely through the formal finite package.

3. **Finite-core branch.**
   The current asymptotic contradiction is pair-like. The general finite-core case remains a separate branch if the first odd coefficient cancels but a higher odd anomaly survives.

4. **Effective threshold and low-height bridge.**
   The endgame currently excludes sufficiently large pair-like anomalies. A complete RH proof would need an effective (Q_*), conversion to a height threshold (T_*), and finite low-height verification or another low-height argument. The draft’s earlier final theorem was correctly downgraded to an asymptotic pair-like branch exclusion.

The proof is no longer stuck at the old UV-026/Gate A/Gate B coefficient audit.

[

\text{Gate A closed.}
}
]

[

}
]

We are **not** at a final proof of RH.

}
]

# Handoff: current proof state and next work

## Executive summary

The paper is no longer blocked at the old UV-026 coefficient-audit layer. The local corrected-package construction is now organized around two distinct gates:

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate A: leading homogeneous cubic audit}}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate B: raw edge-divisibility / first-collision theorem}.}
\]

The current draft should be read as follows:

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate A is closed in aggregate.}}
\]

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate B is conditionally closed through ordinary order }7.}
\]

The next local target is **not** another raw seven-family audit. It is the post-Gate-B connection package:

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0,
\]

where

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]

The next global target is the **package-to-transform / hidden-state / finite-core bridge**, especially the fundamental theorem needed to close the 2-point and mixed 4-point branches.

The proof is not complete. The current endgame is an asymptotic pair-like branch exclusion, not a full RH proof.

---

# 1. Important terminology

## Gate A

Gate A is the leading homogeneous cubic non-\((1,1)\) audit at ordinary order \(7\).

Under the homogeneous scalar-grade convention

\[
\operatorname{Gr}_a r
=
\frac{r^{(a+2)}(h)}{(a+2)!}(t-h)^{a+2},
\]

the leading cubic sector is essentially the \((1,1,5)\)-type sector.

The Gate A families are:

\[
L_1YR_1,\qquad L_2YR_0,\qquad L_0YR_2,
\]

\[
L_2N_0R_1,\qquad L_1N_0R_2,
\]

\[
L_3N_0R_0,\qquad L_0N_0R_3.
\]

Current status:

\[
F_1^{\mathrm{lead\ cubic}}=0.
\]

Mechanism:

1. The \(Y\)-slot families are absent at \(B_7\) under homogeneous scalar grade.
2. The pair

\[
L_2N_0R_1+L_1N_0R_2
\]

cancels in aggregate in \(\mathfrak f\).
3. The pair

\[
L_3N_0R_0+L_0N_0R_3
\]

also cancels in aggregate, assuming third-Fréchet midpoint parity.

This is **aggregate fixed-sector cancellation**, not family-by-family determinant gauge. Do not reopen Gate A unless challenging one of the stated hypotheses.

## Gate B

Gate B is the raw edge-divisibility theorem for the full projected corrected-whitening cross-effect.

The raw cross-effect is:

\[
C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
=
\mathcal W(\mathcal B_2)
-\mathcal W(\mathcal B_1(a_1,h_1))
-\mathcal W(\mathcal B_1(a_2,h_2))
+\mathcal W(\mathcal B_0),
\]

where

\[
\mathcal W(G_-,N,G_+)=G_-^{-1/2}NG_+^{-1/2}.
\]

The projection is applied at matrix/source-bidegree level:

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}.
\]

On the collision chart

\[
h_1=h-\frac{\delta}{2},
\qquad
h_2=h+\frac{\delta}{2},
\qquad
a_2=-a_1(1+\kappa\delta),
\]

Gate B is:

\[
[z^7][\delta^1]\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}=0.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}
=
O(a_1\delta^2)
\]

through ordinary order \(7\) in fixed sector.

Current status:

\[
\boxed{\text{Gate B is conditionally closed through order }7.}
\]

Mechanism:

\[
\text{Gate A aggregate closure}
+
\text{odd total-grade subset-complement cancellation}
+
\text{moving source-frame telescoping}
+
\text{\(2\)-jet affine-boundary removal}.
\]

The hypotheses are important. A valid attack on Gate B now must target one of:

\[
\text{all-subset midpoint parity},
\]

\[
\text{leading \(Y\)-middle parity},
\]

\[
\text{moving source-frame telescoping},
\]

\[
\text{affine boundary removal},
\]

\[
\text{full-tower-before-truncation}.
\]

Do not reopen \(d\ge4\) transfer partitions or lower-shadow lists as independent residuals unless one of those hypotheses is rejected.

---

# 2. Core guardrails

## Determinant scalarization is not the quotient definition

The quotient line is

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

If

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
\qquad
C=uI+vS,
\]

then

\[
uv_5-u_5v
\]

is a good-patch coordinate for the quotient. It is not the definition of the quotient object.

The quotient object is:

\[
[C]\in \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

If

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=0,
\]

the determinant coordinate is unavailable; such tests are quotient-inconclusive.

## \(Z_j[M]\), not \(B_j[M]\), enters the connection formula

After Gate B, the next table is the pre-trivialization edge table

\[
Z_j[M].
\]

The connection formula is:

\[
q_{\mathrm{conn},7}[M]
=
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M],A_i).
\]

Post-trivialization coefficients

\[
B_j[M]
\]

already include the moving-line correction. Do not insert \(B_j[M]\) into the pre-trivialization connection sum; that double-counts the connection.

Also be careful with notation:

\[
Z_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m[z]_{\le7}
\]

is the polynomial table, while

\[
Z_j[M]:=[z^7]Z_j[M](z)\in\mathfrak f_m
\]

is the vector used in the connection formula.

## Raw placement-level vanishing is false

Individual transfer placements can be nonzero in fixed sector. This does not contradict Gate A or Gate B.

The proof uses labelled aggregate cancellation:

\[
I \leftrightarrow J\setminus I,
\]

midpoint parity, symmetry of the relevant coefficients, and transpose-invariance of

\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}.
\]

A nonzero unsymmetrized subchannel is not a counterexample unless it survives the aggregate sum.

## Lower-shadow blanket absorption is not the mechanism

Grades

\[
0,2,4
\]

are not simply discarded. They can appear in the moving Taylor source tower.

The mechanism is:

1. amplitude terms of total grade \(7\) cancel by odd total-grade subset-complement cancellation;
2. source-motion lower terms cancel by moving source-frame telescoping;
3. only the affine grade \(-1\) boundary is removed by \(2\)-jet normalization.

So do not claim “lower shadows are baseline data” as a blanket theorem.

## Post-edge quotient compatibility cannot prove edge divisibility

Do not use

\[
Q_{7,m}^q(C)=Q_{7,m}^q(\Pi_{1,1}C)
\]

as the first proof of Gate B.

Gate B must be raw:

\[
[z^7][\delta^1]\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}=0.
\]

Only after that does the normalized package exist holomorphically.

---

# 3. Local frontier after Gate B

## Main local task

Compute / prove the pre-trivialization connection identities:

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

where

\[
M_A=\dot r_3r_2\dot r_4,
\qquad
M_C=\dot r_3\dot r_2r_4.
\]

The moving-line coefficients are:

\[
A_i=[\epsilon^i]\tau_{h\to m}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h),
\qquad
1\le i\le7,
\]

with

\[
\epsilon=h-m.
\]

The lower pre-trivialization edge coefficients are:

\[
Z_j[M_A],
\qquad
Z_j[M_C],
\qquad
0\le j\le6.
\]

## What \(Z_j[M]\) is

For a residual-source monomial \(M\),

\[
Z_j[M](z)
=
-[\epsilon^j\delta^2M]\,
a_1^{-1}
\tau_{h\to m}
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
(1-\Pi_{1,1})_{\rm mix}C_{\mathcal W}^{\raw}.
\]

Equivalently, after edge normalization,

\[
Z_j[M](z)
=
[\epsilon^jM]\,
\tau_{h\to m}
\pi_{\mathfrak f_h}[z^{\le7}]
\widehat C_{\mathcal W}^{\amp}.
\]

Then

\[
Z_j[M]=[z^7]Z_j[M](z).
\]

## What remains open locally

The paper defines the \(Z_j[M]\) extraction target. It does not yet compute the full finite table for \(M_A\) and \(M_C\).

So the next local theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{derive and evaluate }Z_j[M_A],Z_j[M_C]\text{ and prove the two connection identities.}
}
\]

This is no longer a raw seven-family gate. It is post-Gate-B package-table work.

---

# 4. Downstream global frontier

Even after the \(Z_j\)-connection package closes, the proof is not complete.

The main downstream obligations are:

## 4.1 Package-to-jet / hidden-state bridge

Need a theorem that the corrected package actually feeds the \(N\)-point transform’s first surviving odd coefficient:

\[
\Xi_\zeta^{(N)}
\]

in the required way.

The key warning:

reduced package equality does not automatically imply hidden-state equality.

The reduced package forgets raw septic representative freedom:

\[
A_7^{\mathfrak f}
\longmapsto
A_7^{\mathfrak f}+\chi A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

But

\[
\Phi_K(A_5^{\mathfrak f})=2v_5.
\]

So this forgotten direction is not automatically invisible to \(\Phi_K\) unless \(v_5=0\) or an additional transform-level theorem shows cancellation.

The missing theorem is a state-locality theorem modulo \(\ker\Phi_K\), not merely quotient-septic closure.

## 4.2 Bottleneck C: exceptional-divisor fiber selection

Bottleneck C is not solved by choosing a local septic representative such as

\[
v_7=0,\qquad u_7=0,\qquad T=v_7/c.
\]

Those are diagnostic coordinates.

The C-side object has already quotiented raw septic representatives away. The real C obstruction is the exceptional-divisor trace:

\[
B(m,\kappa):=
\widetilde\Psi^{\corr}_{\mathrm{red}}(m,\kappa,0).
\]

Formal properties like analyticity, swap-evenness, fixed codomain, scalar normalization, and the central value

\[
B(m,0)=\widehat\Psi(m)
\]

do not force

\[
\partial_\kappa B=0.
\]

A C proof must supply actual diagonal merger, same-reduced-image, or collision-functoriality for the corrected two-atom package.

## 4.3 Bottleneck D: hidden \(\Phi_K\)-visible state

The local diagnostic tuple

\[
\widetilde\Psi_{\min}
=
(x,Y,S,T)
=
\left(
\frac{v_5}{c},
\frac{u_5}{c},
\frac{\Delta_7}{cv_5},
\frac{v_7}{c}
\right)
\]

is useful on the good patch

\[
\{c\ne0,\ v_5\ne0\}.
\]

But it is not a canonical global theorem object and does not give finite determination of the first surviving odd jet.

The global target remains:

\[
\text{the first }\Phi_K\text{-visible corrected odd coefficient modulo }\ker\Phi_K.
\]

## 4.4 Finite-core / non-pair-like branch

The final contradiction currently handles the pair-like branch asymptotically.

A more general finite-core branch is still possible:

the first odd coefficient may cancel while a genuine corrected anomaly survives at a higher odd order.

So the proof needs a finite-core lower-model transfer theorem:

either control

\[
\Xi_{\core}^{(N)}
\]

at size comparable to

\[
(x/B_\zeta)S,
\]

or prove a one-sided lower law for the first surviving odd finite-core coefficient and feed it into the positive-kernel \(N\)-point formula.

## 4.5 Effective high-height and low-height bridge

The final contradiction is asymptotic in \(Q\). It excludes sufficiently large pair-like off-line anomalies, but not by itself all low-height off-line zeros.

A full proof would need:

1. explicit threshold \(Q_*\);
2. conversion from \(Q_*\) to height threshold \(T_*\);
3. finite low-height verification or a uniform low-height argument;
4. non-pair-like finite-core branch closure.

The final theorem in the draft should now be read as an asymptotic pair-like branch exclusion, not a completed proof of RH.

---

# 5. What the next agent should do

There are two natural directions.

## Direction A: finish local post-Gate-B connection package

Goal:

\[
R_7^{(A)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_A],A_i)=0,
\]

\[
R_7^{(C)}
+
\sum_{i=1}^{7}\omega_m(Z_{7-i}[M_C],A_i)=0.
\]

Tasks:

1. Extract \(A_i=[\epsilon^i]\tau_{h\to m}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)\), \(1\le i\le7\).
2. Extract \(Z_j[M_A]\), \(Z_j[M_C]\), \(0\le j\le6\), from the edge-normalized package.
3. Prove or compute the connection identities.
4. Do not use \(B_j[M]\) in the connection formula.
5. Do not substitute raw seven-family coefficients for \(Z_j[M]\).
6. Keep pre-trivialization and post-trivialization representatives distinct.

This is the most concrete local continuation.

## Direction B: fundamental theorem / package-to-transform bridge

Goal:

build the theorem that lets the corrected package close the 2-point and mixed 4-point branches globally.

Tasks:

1. Formalize the package-to-\(\Xi_\zeta^{(N)}\) hidden-state bridge.
2. Determine exactly what data from the local package are visible under \(\Phi_K\).
3. Prove state-locality modulo \(\ker\Phi_K\).
4. Prove diagonal merger / exceptional-divisor fiber selection.
5. Show that non-\((1,1)\) corrected source terms cannot create an independent mixed 4-point carrier after Gate B.
6. Connect the first surviving corrected odd package coefficient to a finite-core lower model.

This is the more fundamental direction and is probably where 2-point / mixed 4-point closure will ultimately happen.

---

# 6. Recommended immediate agent split

I would split agents like this.

## Agent 1 — \(Z_j\)-connection package

Prompt target:

\[
Z_j[M_A],\quad Z_j[M_C],\quad A_i,\quad R_7^{(A)},\quad R_7^{(C)}.
\]

Ask them to compute or reduce the two connection identities. They should work only post-Gate-B and use pre-trivialization \(Z_j\), not \(B_j\).

## Agent 2 — package-to-transform hidden-state bridge

Prompt target:

\[
\text{Does quotient package equality imply }\Phi_K\text{-visible hidden-state equality?}
\]

They should focus on the affine freedom

\[
A_7^{\mathfrak f}\mapsto A_7^{\mathfrak f}+\chi A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

and the fact that

\[
\Phi_K(A_5^{\mathfrak f})=2v_5.
\]

They should try to prove state-locality modulo \(\ker\Phi_K\), or produce a precise obstruction.

## Agent 3 — Bottleneck C / diagonal merger

Prompt target:

\[
B(m,\kappa)=\widetilde\Psi^{\corr}_{\mathrm{red}}(m,\kappa,0).
\]

They should prove or refute

\[
\partial_\kappa B=0
\]

from actual corrected-package functoriality, not formal properties.

## Agent 4 — finite-core lower model

Prompt target:

\[
\Xi_{\core}^{(N)}
\]

or a one-sided lower law for the first surviving odd finite-core coefficient.

They should not assume pair-like first odd behavior.

---

# 7. Common mistakes to avoid

Do not:

1. reopen Gate A unless attacking a stated hypothesis;
2. reopen \(d\ge4\) amplitude partitions unless attacking all-subset midpoint parity;
3. treat lower shadows \(0,2,4\) as discarded baseline data;
4. use \(B_j[M]\) in the connection sum;
5. use post-edge quotient compatibility to prove raw edge divisibility;
6. use determinant scalarization as the quotient definition;
7. treat raw seven-family numerical failures as failures of the corrected package theorem;
8. claim the final theorem proves RH rather than asymptotic pair-like exclusion;
9. confuse \(Z_j[M](z)\) with \(Z_j[M]=[z^7]Z_j[M](z)\);
10. forget that \(A_i\) in the connection formula means the transported coefficient

\[
A_i=[\epsilon^i]\tau_{h\to m}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h).
\]

---

}
\]

\boxed{
\text{Gate A closed; Gate B conditionally closed through order }7.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{next local theorem: }Z_j\text{-connection cancellation.}
}
\]

\[

\text{next fundamental theorem: package-to-transform hidden-state bridge.}

We are **not** done.

But the frontier is now much sharper than before. The old local coefficient gates are no longer the main obstruction. The main work is now:

\[

+
\text{global package-to-transform / finite-core bridge}.
}
\]
